Fix: fetch the branch ref explicitly ('git fetch origin +refs/heads/
:refs/remotes/origin/
') and/or use an explicit lease ('--force-with-lease=
:'). To reconcile custom hooks with a passing check: keep custom hooks (e.g. session-start-stamp) in SEPARATE sibling entries with the same matcher, and let 'bd setup ' own its marker block instead of hand-writing pointers. mybd repo now passes both checks; regression harness: scripts/test-agent-hooks. ### beads-cli-architecture-trap-learned-mybd-rlpt-pr Beads CLI architecture trap (learned mybd-rlpt / PR gastownhall/beads#4654, 2026-07-07): schema migrations and the remote-migrate gate run at STORE OPEN inside root PersistentPreRunE (cmd/bd/main.go, newDoltStore + autoMigrateOnVersionBump), long before any subcommand's RunE. Two implications for future beads work: (1) any flag that must influence migration/gate behavior must be consulted in the root prerun (pattern: isForcedMigrate / isWorkingSetReconcileCommand), not in the command's RunE; (2) preview flags like --dry-run/--inspect do NOT protect against migration side effects because MigrateUp already ran at open - combinations that override the gate must be rejected BEFORE open. Cross-vendor review datapoint: claude opus reviewer explicitly assessed --force+--dry-run as harmless; codex gpt-5.5 traced the store-open path and caught it. (close completed issues) [ ] 2. run quality gates (tests, linters, builds when relevant) [ ] 3. git status (check what changed) [ ] 4. team-maintainer: commit, sync, push as part of routine work (unless current instructions say otherwise) ``` **Policy:** agent.profile=team-maintainer is active. Commit, sync, and push as part of routine work; explicit "do not commit"/"do not push" instructions still override. ## Core Rules - **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`) - **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking - **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting - **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories `. - Persistence you don't need beats lost context - Profile: team-maintainer active (agent.profile=team-maintainer) - commit, sync, and push are routine; explicit no-commit/no-push instructions still override. - Git workflow: team-maintainer active - commit/push are routine unless explicitly restricted - Session management: check `bd ready` for available work ## Essential Commands ### Finding Work - `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers) - `bd list --status=open` - All open issues - `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work - `bd show ` - Detailed issue view with dependencies ### Creating & Updating - `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low" - `bd create ... --parent=` - Hierarchical child (task under epic, subtask under task; inherits parent labels) - `bd update --claim` - Claim work - `bd unclaim ` - Release stuck issue (agent crashed) - `bd update --assignee=username` - Assign to someone - `bd update --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline - `bd close ` - Mark complete - `bd close ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient) - `bd close --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason - **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency - **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents ### Dependencies & Blocking - `bd dep add ` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on) - `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues - `bd show ` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue ### Sync & Collaboration - `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote - `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote - `bd search ` - Search issues by keyword ### Project Health - `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts) - `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks) - `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans) ### Quality Tools - `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections - `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate) - `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions - `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes - `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create - `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections ### Lifecycle & Hygiene - `bd defer --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date - `bd supersede --with=` - Mark issue as superseded - `bd close --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing - `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity - `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies - `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans) - `bd human ` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss) ### Structured Workflows - `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates - `bd mol pour ` - Start structured workflow from formula ## Common Workflows **Starting work:** ```bash bd ready # Find available work bd show # Review issue details bd update --claim # Claim it ``` **Completing work:** ```bash bd close ... # Close all completed issues at once git status # Check changed files # team-maintainer: commit, sync, push are routine unless instructions forbid it git add . && git commit -m "..." bd dolt push git push ``` **Creating dependent work:** ```bash # Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items) bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests) ``` > **Note**: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are independent files (not symlinked and not sharing an inode). Mirror substantive edits across both, or symlink one to the other. "}}

message.content:
For items filed by quad341, run a PR/issue health sweep and drive everything to done. Quad has followed up on comments and reviews.

1. Survey first, then act: list open PRs on gastownhall/beads (and mybd),
   with CI, mergeable state, review state, and staleness. Report the map
   before changing anything.

2. Then work every PR to resolution under these standing rules:
   - reviewed + concerns addressed + CI green -> preflight, squash-merge,
     comment/close linked issues
   - reviews with unaddressed concerns or red CI -> dispatch workers to fix
   - no reviews -> dispatch reviewers; agent reviews count as "reviewed"
   - storage/migration/gate-adjacent changes get paired cross-vendor review
     (opus + codex); adjudicate contradictions against pinned source or repo
     history before posting; keep review rounds going until a pass comes
     back clean
   - workers verify a reviewer's factual claims before implementing, and
     STOP at defect boundaries or scope changes instead of improvising

3. Delegation defaults: builders in worktrees, tiered models, resume the
   same agent for follow-up rounds instead of respawning, background
   everything and act on notifications. Track discovered work as beads
   (discovered-from links), bd remember durable lessons, sync dolt.

4. Ask me only for: destructive/irreversible calls, scope changes, and
   before dispatching workers onto PRs I authored in another session.
   Everything else, proceed and report outcomes as they land.

Check bd memories for cross-vendor-review-pairing, dolt-cli-prepare-execute-
ddl-noop, and reviewer-verify-before-filing-blockers before reviewing or
testing migrations.

---

above prompt was composed for generic agents, adjust as needed for your own strengths and weaknesses.

addedLines:
- builder: Mid-tier implementer for well-scoped changes with a clear spec: apply a planned edit, write a test from a described behavior, mechanical refactors, doc/report updates. Give it exact files and acceptance criteria. Not for open-ended design or ambiguous debugging - keep those in the orchestrator session.
 (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write, Bash)
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- nori-change-documenter: Creates documentation about changes to a codebase. Call the nori-change-documenter whenever you change the codebase. Provide all of the relevance context for *why* the change was made. Be extremely detailed! Provide as much context as possible so the agent can accurately update documentation. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Write, Edit, Bash)

- nori-codebase-analyzer: Analyzes codebase implementation details. Call the nori-codebase-analyzer agent when you need to find detailed information about specific components. As always, the more detailed your request prompt, the better! :) (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS)

- nori-codebase-locator: Locates files, directories, and components relevant to a feature or task. Call `nori-codebase-locator` with human language prompt describing what you're looking for. Basically a "Super Grep/Glob/LS tool" — Use it if you find yourself desiring to use one of these tools more than once. (Tools: Grep, Glob, LS)

- nori-codebase-pattern-finder: nori-codebase-pattern-finder is a useful subagent_type for finding similar implementations, usage examples, or existing patterns that can be modeled after. It will give you concrete code examples based on what you're looking for! It's sorta like nori-codebase-locator, but it will not only tell you the location of files, it will also give you code details! (Tools: Grep, Glob, Read, LS)

- nori-initial-documenter: Creates documentation about a codebase. This agent creates the initial documentation for the codebase; use this agent when you want to create documentation and no existing documentation is present. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Write, Edit, Bash)

- nori-web-search-researcher: Do you find yourself desiring information that you don't quite feel well-trained (confident) on? Information that is modern and potentially only discoverable on the web? Use the nori-web-search-researcher subagent_type today to find any and all answers to your questions! It will research deeply to figure out and attempt to answer your questions! If you aren't immediately satisfied you can get your money back! (Not really - but you can re-run nori-web-search-researcher with an altered prompt in the event you're not satisfied the first time) (Tools: WebSearch, WebFetch, TodoWrite, Read, Grep, Glob, LS)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- reviewer: High-judgment read-only review. Use to check a diff or design for correctness bugs, edge cases, and simpler alternatives before integrating - especially work produced by builder agents. Returns findings with file:line references and severity; does not fix anything.
 (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- scout: Cheap, fast read-only recon, executed on GPT-5.6 Terra (medium reasoning) via the Codex CLI. Use for searches, file inventories, "where is X defined/used", summarizing a file or directory, running read-only bd/git commands or tests and reporting output verbatim. No editing. Delegate here whenever the task is mechanical lookup rather than judgment. Prefer calling `scripts/codex-agent scout` directly from the orchestrator when you can (same isolation, no relay hop; include the recon rules from this file in the prompt); this agent type is the interface for workflow agentType calls and the fallback when codex is unavailable.
 (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- quota-watch: Monitor Amp Free quota and handoff before exhaustion. Use when user says quota-watch, check quota, or monitor usage.
- initialize-noridocs: Initialize docs.md files throughout the codebase
- nori-create-skillset: Create a new custom skillset by cloning an existing skillset
- nori-switch-skillset: Switch between Nori configuration skillsets (amol, senior-swe, product-manager, documenter, none)
- session-close: Cold-start-readiness check before ending a work session - runs the 3 judgment prompts plus the mechanical backstop, then the standard close/push.
- setup-beads-sync: Set up a private GitHub Dolt repo for personal beads and configure sync for the current project
- switch-nori-profile: Switch between Nori configuration profiles (amol, senior-swe, product-manager, documenter, none)
- triage: Layer 2 issue triage - mirror upstream into bd, classify each stub to a one-line disposition, emit a short digest. Never posts text upstream.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Observed 2026-07-23 rebasing PRs gastownhall/beads#4761 and #4878. 26 27 ### bd-setup-codex-claude-check-require-byte-exact 28 bd setup codex/claude --check require BYTE-EXACT bd-managed marker sections () in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and canonical per-entry hook JSON (one canonical command per entry; extra commands in the same entry break codexHookEntriesContain equality). To reconcile custom hooks with a passing check: keep custom hooks (e.g. session-start-stamp) in SEPARATE sibling entries with the same matcher, and let 'bd setup ' own its marker block instead of hand-writing pointers. mybd repo now passes both checks; regression harness: scripts/test-agent-hooks. 29 30 ### beads-cli-architecture-trap-learned-mybd-rlpt-pr 31 Beads CLI architecture trap (learned mybd-rlpt / PR gastownhall/beads#4654, 2026-07-07): schema migrations and the remote-migrate gate run at STORE OPEN inside root PersistentPreRunE (cmd/bd/main.go, newDoltStore + autoMigrateOnVersionBump), long before any subcommand's RunE. Two implications for future beads work: (1) any flag that must influence migration/gate behavior must be consulted in the root prerun (pattern: isForcedMigrate / isWorkingSetReconcileCommand), not in the command's RunE; (2) preview flags like --dry-run/--inspect do NOT protect against migration side effects because MigrateUp already ran at open - combinations that override the gate must be rejected BEFORE open. Cross-vendor review datapoint: claude opus reviewer explicitly assessed --force+--dry-run as harmless; codex gpt-5.5 traced the store-open path and caught it. Keep pairing reviewers across vendors on gate/store-open changes. 32 33 34 # 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨 35 36 **CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist: 37 38 ``` 39 [ ] 1. bd close ... (close completed issues) 40 [ ] 2. run quality gates (tests, linters, builds when relevant) 41 [ ] 3. git status (check what changed) 42 [ ] 4. team-maintainer: commit, sync, push as part of routine work (unless current instructions say otherwise) 43 ``` 44 45 **Policy:** agent.profile=team-maintainer is active. Commit, sync, and push as part of routine work; explicit "do not commit"/"do not push" instructions still override. 46 47 ## Core Rules 48 - **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`) 49 - **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking 50 - **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting 51 - **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories `. 52 - Persistence you don't need beats lost context 53 - Profile: team-maintainer active (agent.profile=team-maintainer) - commit, sync, and push are routine; explicit no-commit/no-push instructions still override. 54 - Git workflow: team-maintainer active - commit/push are routine unless explicitly restricted 55 - Session management: check `bd ready` for available work 56 57 ## Essential Commands 58 59 ### Finding Work 60 - `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers) 61 - `bd list --status=open` - All open issues 62 - `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work 63 - `bd show ` - Detailed issue view with dependencies 64 65 ### Creating & Updating 66 - `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue 67 - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low" 68 - `bd create ... --parent=` - Hierarchical child (task under epic, subtask under task; inherits parent labels) 69 - `bd update --claim` - Claim work 70 - `bd unclaim ` - Release stuck issue (agent crashed) 71 - `bd update --assignee=username` - Assign to someone 72 - `bd update --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline 73 - `bd close ` - Mark complete 74 - `bd close ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient) 75 - `bd close --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason 76 - **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency 77 - **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents 78 79 ### Dependencies & Blocking 80 - `bd dep add ` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on) 81 - `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues 82 - `bd show ` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue 83 84 ### Sync & Collaboration 85 - `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote 86 - `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote 87 - `bd search ` - Search issues by keyword 88 89 ### Project Health 90 - `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts) 91 - `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks) 92 - `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans) 93 94 ### Quality Tools 95 - `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections 96 - `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate) 97 - `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions 98 - `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes 99 - `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create 100 - `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections 101 102 ### Lifecycle & Hygiene 103 - `bd defer --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date 104 - `bd supersede --with=` - Mark issue as superseded 105 - `bd close --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing 106 - `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity 107 - `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies 108 - `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans) 109 - `bd human ` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss) 110 111 ### Structured Workflows 112 - `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates 113 - `bd mol pour ` - Start structured workflow from formula 114 115 ## Common Workflows 116 117 **Starting work:** 118 ```bash 119 bd ready # Find available work 120 bd show # Review issue details 121 bd update --claim # Claim it 122 ``` 123 124 **Completing work:** 125 ```bash 126 bd close ... # Close all completed issues at once 127 git status # Check changed files 128 # team-maintainer: commit, sync, push are routine unless instructions forbid it 129 git add . && git commit -m "..." 130 bd dolt push 131 git push 132 ``` 133 134 **Creating dependent work:** 135 ```bash 136 # Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items) 137 bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature 138 bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task 139 bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests) 140 ``` 141 142 > **Note**: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are independent files (not symlinked and not sharing an inode). Mirror substantive edits across both, or symlink one to the other. 143

Update in place with `bd remember --key "new content"`. Search with `bd memories `. Remove with `bd forget `. > 64 more memories are not shown here (capped by max-memory-chars=4500). Browse the full set with `bd memories ` or recall one with `bd remember `. ### agent-profile-team-maintainer Owner directive (maphew, 2026-07-08): the conservative 'do not commit/push/dolt-sync without explicit authority' default is NOT wanted in this repo. agent.profile=team-maintainer is set via bd config (config.yaml); bd prime >= c52c23060 emits the team-maintainer session-close wording (commit, sync, push as routine work; an explicit in-prompt 'do not push' still overrides). If a machine's bd binary predates gastownhall/beads#4585 the key is silently ignored and prime falls back to conservative wording - upgrade the binary rather than re-adding approval gates. ### bd-binary-provenance Do not trust 'bd version' to identify the beads-source commit of a locally built binary: at runtime it reports the git HEAD of the WORKSPACE repo it runs in (e.g. 'dev: main@' when run inside the mybd coordination repo or a clone of it), not the commit it was built from. Verify build provenance with 'go version -m ' (embedded module list, incl. the exact dolthub/dolt/go pseudo-version) or record 'git rev-parse HEAD' at build time. Observed 2026-07-15 while A/B-testing dolt v2.2.0 builds for mybd-sk7e. ### bd-doctor-embedded-policy bd doctor embedded-mode policy (coffeegoddd, storage-layer owner — authoritative): doctor stays stubbed in embedded mode deliberately; enable ONE subcommand at a time, human-vetted, safe checks first (PR #3758 pattern: artifacts/conventions/pollution). No database-layer checks or --fix against embedded dolt from beads code — long-term users/agents never touch the DB layer at all (driver-interface roadmap, see beads-storage-driver-roadmap memory + upstream AGENTS.md Storage Boundary). Canonical record: gastownhall/beads#3794 (scoping directive) + #3597 (pain reports). The doctor.go stub itself carries no explanatory comment as of 2026-07-14 — do not 'fix' the stub wholesale; follow the per-subcommand path. ### bd-main-worktree-force-lease-quirk bd-main worktrees: 'git push --force-with-lease' (implicit lease) can spuriously fail with 'stale info' on topic branches because the clone's remote.origin.fetch refspec is sparse (tracks only main), so fetch never refreshes refs/remotes/origin/ used for lease negotiation. Fix: fetch the branch ref explicitly ('git fetch origin +refs/heads/
:refs/remotes/origin/
') and/or use an explicit lease ('--force-with-lease=
:'). input.command: bd remember cross-vendor-review-pairing; echo ===; bd remember dolt-cli-prepare-execute-ddl-noop; echo ===; bd remember reviewer-verify-before-filing-blockers
input.description: Recall three bd memories about review/migration practices

content.content:
(recalled "cross-vendor-review-pairing" -- a bare existing key READS. To overwrite: `bd remember "<new content>" --key cross-vendor-review-pairing`)
Cross-vendor review pairing keeps paying for storage/gate changes - the vendors reliably find DISJOINT defects. Confirmed datapoints: (1) #4654 (2026-07-07): opus missed the store-open migration side effect, codex caught it. (2) #4986 initial review: opus caught the PATH-dolt unknown-flag regression; codex caught Beads-managed sql-servers inheriting Dolt auto-GC zstd defaults which opus asserted did not exist. (3) #4987: opus clean, codex found two majors (constraints over dirty data). (4) #4986 delta review: codex caught setupDoltConfig parent-.doltcfg discovery running ONLY in flag mode. (5) 2026-07-23 PR-health sweep: opus-alone would have APPROVED #4804 (codex found the mid-pass cursor-commit interleaving that defeats the lock-skip probe) and #4918 (codex found the --file/--graph batch route bypassing the new validation); on #4985 the majors were disjoint (opus: PostRun tip auto-commit; codex: proxied bypass + writable dry-run target); on #4997 both converged on audit-in-retryable-tx (convergence = near-certain). Codex has caught the decisive storage-layer major in most pairings; opus contributes precise verification and different majors. Adjudication rules: verify each vendor's factual claims against the PR head before posting (see reviewer-verify-before-filing-blockers); single-vendor blockers get traced and sometimes downgraded (codex's 4997 stale-snapshot blocker was inherited embedded semantics); merge into ONE posted review naming who found what, signed as adjudicator with both vendor models.
===
(recalled "dolt-cli-prepare-execute-ddl-noop" -- a bare existing key READS. To overwrite: `bd remember "<new content>" --key dolt-cli-prepare-execute-ddl-noop`)
dolt CLI trap (v2.2.2, observed 2026-07-23, mybd-i9un): 'dolt sql -f' and '-q' SILENTLY no-op any PREPARE ... FROM @sql; EXECUTE stmt; driven ALTER TABLE - no error, DDL just does not apply. Plain non-prepared ALTER works. Beads frozen migration files use exactly this guarded pattern (SET @sql = IF(...); PREPARE; EXECUTE), so the schema_test.go ThroughDoltCLI harness can only ever exercise the NO-OP branches of frozen guarded DDL; every existing 'repairs X' CLI test uses hand-written substitutes (cli_migrations.go) or Go-level DDL instead. Production is unaffected (MigrateUp goes through database/sql + go-mysql-server, never dolt CLI). To genuinely test a frozen migration's guarded branch, execute the frozen .up.sql through a real engine (embedded store pattern), not the CLI. Possibly a reportable dolthub/dolt bug (silent failure).
===
(recalled "reviewer-verify-before-filing-blockers" -- a bare existing key READS. To overwrite: `bd remember "<new content>" --key reviewer-verify-before-filing-blockers`)
Reviewer claims must be verified before posting/acting - datapoints from both directions: (a) 2026-07-23 #4997: a strong opus review asserted the new proxied integration test was DORMANT in CI; refuted by reading the shard script at PR head (TestProxiedServer* hash-distribute into live shards). (b) 2026-07-23 #4976 sweep: an opus/sonnet reviewer filed a blocker claiming documented import-chunking didn't exist; it did (importIssuesChunked, landed #4729) - reviewer traced only the small-import path; the wrong finding was posted publicly and needed retraction. (c) codex's #4997 'stale snapshot close' blocker downgraded to design note after checking the embedded path has identical semantics. Method: for every blocker/should-fix, re-trace the specific file:line at the PR head (git fetch upstream pull/N/head) and check whether the claimed-broken behavior differs from main/siblings BEFORE posting or dispatching workers; give fixer agents an explicit verify-claim-first STOP condition; order review sweeps verify-then-post. Related: pr-body-wiring-claims-verify-by-grep, verify-cheap-scout-claims-before-recording, cross-vendor-review-pairing.

content.content:
===== PR 4213 =====
--- REVIEW by maphew [COMMENTED] 2026-07-05T14:05:54Z:
Thanks for this - a 27-line, test-only patch that makes `internal/storage/domain/fs`'s suite hermetic by chdir-ing to a fresh non-git temp dir and calling `git.ResetCaches()` in `SetupSuite`/`TearDownSuite`. Cheap insurance against a real class of hazard (a process-wide `sync.Once` cache in `internal/git` picking up worktree state from wherever the test binary happens to start), and it only touches `suite_test.go` - no product code, no storage-boundary concerns.

Findings:

- [minor] I could not reproduce the clobber described in the PR body against current `upstream/main`. I built a scratch main-checkout + linked-worktree pair, seeded a sentinel `.beads/metadata.json` in the main checkout, and ran `go test ./internal/storage/domain/fs/ -run TestDomainFS -v` from the linked worktree without this patch applied - the sentinel was untouched. Tracing the call graph: `resolveBeadsDir` (`internal/storage/domain/fs/beads.go`) calls `beads.ResolveBeadsDirForRepo(workDir)`, which resolves worktree fallback via `worktreeFallbackBeadsDirForRepo` - a *repoPath*-scoped helper (`git -C repoPath ...`) added by #3265 (2026-04-24), not the process-cwd-cached `git.IsWorktree()`/`GetWorktreeFallbackBeadsDir()` the PR blames. Since `newRepo()` also always passes a fresh `t.TempDir()` (not a git repo) as `workDir`, the `git -C` call fails harmlessly and falls back to the local temp `.beads`. It's possible #3265 already closed this hole before this branch was cut (2026-05-28), or the original gm-q6biba incident hit a different path than the current `readBeadsConfigParses` fixture does today. Worth a quick sanity check on your end against latest `main` - but either way the fix is harmless defense-in-depth even if the original path is no longer live, so this isn't a reason to hold the PR.
- [nit] No `t.Parallel()` is used anywhere in this package, so the suite-wide chdir is safe for sequential execution as written; if a future subtest in this package adds parallelism this pattern would nee
--- COMMENT by codecov-commenter 2026-05-28T02:58:58Z:

This PR pairs well with the merged #4152 and #4531 schema-skew guard implementation—the documentation is accurate, timely, and user-friendly.

**Verification:**
- Error message in README matches the `SchemaSkewError.UserMessage()` implementation on upstream/main exactly
- Bypass mechanism (`BD_IGNORE_SCHEMA_SKEW=1` / `--ignore-schema-skew`) is correctly documented with both forms shown in the example
- "When this fires" section accurately describes the guard (DB schema *ahead* of binary only; fresh DBs and normal forward migrations unaffected)

**One note for context:** The PR correctly references that the designer spec (be-x0rl6) recommended a longer description for the `--ignore-schema-skew` flag, but notes this belongs in #4152. That PR has since merged with the shorter description; a follow-up flag-help improvement could land separately if desired. This docs PR makes no claim to address it, so not a blocker.

Recommendation: **Merge as-is.** This brings user-facing docs into sync with the implemented guard.

Thank you for the clear documentation!

--- COMMENT by quad341 2026-05-08T18:36:18Z:
**be-szr verification (rig investigator)**

Code-reading confirms the fix is correct and bounded:

- `internal/utils/id_parser.go:53-60` — new `store.GetIssue(input)` fallback after the `SearchIssues` fast path.
- `internal/storage/postgres/issues.go:144-159` — `PostgresStore.GetIssue` already falls through `issues` → `wisps` on `storage.ErrNotFound`, so the fallback finds mail wisps.
- `internal/storage/issueops/get_issue.go:16-38` — `GetIssueInTx` (used by Dolt) routes via `IsActiveWispInTx`, so Dolt finds wisps in the fast path and the new fallback is a no-op there.

Minor wording note on the PR description: PG's `SearchIssues` does call `searchTable` against the wisps table when `filter.Ephemeral` is nil (`postgres/issues.go:373-397`), but the per-row `(ephemeral = FALSE OR ephemeral IS NULL)` guard added at `searchTable` lines 462-470 filters out mail wisps (which carry `ephemeral=true`). So the fast-path miss is real, just for a slightly more specific reason than "only consults the issues table". Doesn't change the fix — just flagging in case the description is reused elsewhere.

**Filing follow-up** for the explicit out-of-scope item (partial wisp IDs on PG): the wisp fallback at `id_parser.go:167-196` calls `SearchIssues` with `Ephemeral=true`. PG's `SearchIssues` with `Ephemeral=true` searches the issues table only (with `ephemeral=true` clause → 0 rows), while Dolt's `SearchIssuesInTx` (`internal/storage/issueops/search.go:17-26`) routes Ephemeral=true to the wisps 
--- COMMENT by quad341 2026-05-19T20:15:39Z:

- **Correctness**: `SearchIssues` only queries the issues table; on PG, wisps live separately. `GetIssue`'s PG implementation falls through to wisps on NotFound — using it as a fallback is the right approach. ✓
- **Error handling**: `err == nil && issue != nil` guard is correct. ✓
- **Comment**: Explains the PG-specific constraint clearly for future readers. ✓
- **CI**: All checks green, MERGEABLE/CLEAN. ✓

Ready to merge.

--- COMMENT by quad341 2026-05-04T22:18:45Z:
**CI flake analysis (Test Nix Flake — only failing check):**

The cancelled `Test Nix Flake` job on run [25343492072](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/actions/runs/25343492072/job/74306528541) is a transient infrastructure flake, not a code issue with this PR. Logs show the step "Run bd help via Nix" hit the 10-minute step timeout while the nix daemon kept retrying narinfo fetches against `install.determinate.systems` and getting back `HTTP/2 stream … was not closed cleanly: INTERNAL_ERROR`:

```
warning: error: unable to download 'https://install.determinate.systems/8lmj961bsxjxnzv0hglmkbar4f9zq4j7.narinfo':
  Stream error in the HTTP/2 framing layer (92) … INTERNAL_ERROR (err 2); retrying in 265 ms
… retrying in 264 ms / 510 ms / 1355 ms / 2353 ms …
##[error]The operation was canceled.
```

The job's own preamble printed the matching status banner from Determinate Systems (`🔵 identified: One high and one moderate vulnerabilities fixed in Determinate Nix … See: https://status.determinate.systems/`), so there was an active incident on their CDN at run time.

Every other check passed green: ubuntu/macos/windows test matrices, the 20 embedded-dolt cmd shards, embedded-dolt storage, lint, formatting, build-tag policy, doc-flags freshness, version-consistency, and the cross-version smoke tests. content.content:
mybd-woa8
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# codex-agent - run OpenAI Codex CLI non-interactively at a named delegation tier.
#
# Maps the repo's subagent tiers (AGENTS.md "Agent Delegation") onto
# `codex exec` model/sandbox/reasoning defaults so Claude Code, Codex, and
# humans all delegate to Codex the same way:
#
#   scout    read-only recon        gpt-5.6-terra, medium reasoning, ephemeral
#   builder  scoped implementation  gpt-5.6-terra, medium reasoning, workspace-write
#   reviewer correctness review     gpt-5.6-sol, high reasoning, read-only
#
# GPT-5.6 family: sol = flagship, terra = balanced lower-cost (mini-like),
# luna = high-volume (nano-like); the bare `gpt-5.6` alias routes to sol.
# GPT-5.6 adds `xhigh` and `max` reasoning efforts - raise a single run with
# e.g. -c model_reasoning_effort='"xhigh"' for the hardest reviews.
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/codex-agent <scout|builder|reviewer> [-C <dir>] [extra codex flags] "<prompt>"
#   scripts/codex-agent reviewer --diff [--base <branch> | --uncommitted | --commit <sha>]
#     (a custom focus prompt is only accepted without those range flags)
#   echo "<prompt>" | scripts/codex-agent scout -
#
# Extra flags are passed through to codex verbatim (e.g. -o FILE to capture
# the final message, --json for JSONL events, --output-schema FILE for
# structured output, -m/-c to override the tier defaults).
#
# Guard rails:
#   - builder must target a linked worktree, never a main checkout
#     (CODEX_AGENT_ALLOW_ROOT=1 overrides, mirroring MYBD_ALLOW_ROOT_COMMIT).
#   - non-interactive codex never prompts for approval, so the sandbox mode
#     is always set explicitly here.

set -euo pipefail

usage() { sed -n '2,30p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//'; }

tier="${1:-}"
case "$tier" in
  scout|builder|reviewer) shift ;;
  -h|--help|"") usage; exit 0 ;;
  *) echo "codex-agent: unknown tier '$tier' (scout|builder|reviewer)" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac

workdir="$PWD"
args=("$@")
for i in "${!args[@]}"; do
  case "${args[$i]}" in
    -C|--cd) workdir="${args[$((i+1))]:-$workdir}" ;;
    --cd=?*) workdir="${args[$i]#--cd=}" ;;
    -C?*)    workdir="${args[$i]#-C}" ;;
  esac
done

# reviewer --diff routes to `codex review` (structured diff review) instead
# of a free-form exec prompt. `codex review` takes no -C/--cd flag, so the
# wrapper strips it and cd's to the target worktree itself.
diff_mode=0
if [ "$tier" = reviewer ]; then
  for a in "$@"; do [ "$a" = "--diff" ] && diff_mode=1; done
fi

input.prompt:
You are a maintainer's worker agent on the beads project. Task: produce the "beads-only minimal repro" that maintainer coffeegoddd requested on gastownhall/beads PR #3813 "fix(utils): be-szr ResolvePartialID falls through to GetIssue for wisps (PG)" by quad341 (+9/-0 in internal/utils/id_parser.go; MERGEABLE, CI green).

Context: The fix adds a store.GetIssue(input) fallback in ResolvePartialID after the SearchIssues fast path, because on the Postgres backend SearchIssues misses mail wisps (they live in a separate wisps table / carry ephemeral=true which the search filters out), while PostgresStore.GetIssue falls through issues → wisps on ErrNotFound. coffeegoddd (2026-05-10): "We need a beads-only minimal repro of the bug, otherwise this is a bug with your orchestration layer." A later maphew comment (2026-06-16) also asked for a concrete repro recipe. quad341 posted code-reading verification but no runnable repro exists on the PR yet.

Working setup: beads clone at /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main (origin=maphew/beads, upstream=gastownhall/beads). Create your own worktree: git -C /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main fetch upstream pull/3813/head:pr-3813-repro && git -C /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main worktree add ../.worktrees/beads/pr-3813-repro pr-3813-repro. Never work in the bd-main root checkout. Build with CGO_ENABLED=1 -tags gms_pure_go (a plain build fails on a missing ICU header).

Steps:
1. Read the full PR thread (gh pr view 3813 --repo gastownhall/beads --json body,comments,reviews) and the patch itself.
2. Investigate how this repo tests the Postgres backend: grep internal/storage/postgres for test setup (env vars like BEADS_TEST_POSTGRES/POSTGRES_DSN, testcontainers, CI workflow steps). Determine the cheapest way to run a real PG-backed test locally. Check whether podman or docker exists on this machine (command -v podman docker) and whether a postgres server is already running (pg_isready, port 5432).
3. Build a MINIMAL repro that a maintainer can run with stock beads only — ideally either (a) a short shell recipe: bd init with postgres backend, create a mail wisp, run `bd show <wisp-id>` → "not found" pre-fix vs resolves post-fix; or (b) a Go test in the existing PG test harness exercising ResolvePartialID with a wisp. Prefer whichever matches existing repo precedent (look at scripts/repro-* directories for the established repro-script pattern: repro.sh + REPRO.md).
4. RUN the repro both ways if PG infra is available: at the PR head (expect pass/resolves) and at merge-base/main without the patch (expect the miss). Record exact commands and output.
5. If the repro genuinely demonstrates the bug, commit it to the PR branch (scripts/repro-be-szr-pg-wisp/ following the repro precedent), push to the contributor branch: git push https://github.com/quad341/beads-sec003-contrib.git HEAD:fix/be-szr-pg-wisp-resolve — commit message trailer from: AGENT_MODEL=sonnet AGENT_REASONING=medium /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/scripts/agent-sig.sh claude --trailer. Then post a signed PR comment addressed to the repro request with the recipe and observed pre/post-fix output (body to a file, run /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/scripts/gh-body-lint <file>, post via gh pr comment 3813 --repo gastownhall/beads --body-file; sign _claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_).
6. STOP conditions (report, don't improvise): no way to run Postgres locally (no container runtime, no server) — in that case still write the repro script + expected output and report it back WITHOUT pushing or posting, flagging it unverified; OR your repro shows the bug does NOT reproduce (fast path finds the wisp) — that contradicts the PR's premise, report with evidence and post nothing; OR the fix itself looks wrong while testing.

Final report (raw data): PG infra found/used, repro commands + verbatim key output pre/post fix, what you pushed/posted (links), or the STOP reason with evidence.

lastPrompt: For items filed by quad341, run a PR/issue health sweep and drive everything to done. Quad has followed up on comments and reviews.  1. Survey first, then act: list open PRs on gastownhall/beads (and…

aiTitle: Health sweep for quad341 PRs on beads

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'quad341-pr-dossiers',
  description: 'Build per-PR state dossiers for quad341 open PRs on gastownhall/beads',
  phases: [{ title: 'Dossier', detail: 'one reader agent per PR' }],
}

const PRS = [3663, 3906, 3908, 3919, 3920, 3971, 4022, 4029, 4045, 4055, 4159, 4160, 4226, 4345]

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['number', 'category', 'storageAdjacent', 'concernsAddressed', 'evidence', 'nextAction'],
  properties: {
    number: { type: 'integer' },
    title: { type: 'string' },
    category: { type: 'string', enum: ['merge-ready', 'needs-rebase', 'needs-review', 'needs-worker', 'blocked-on-human', 'close-candidate', 'wait-ci'] },
    storageAdjacent: { type: 'boolean', description: 'touches internal/storage, migrations, schema, dolt internals, or gate/store-open behavior' },
    stackDeps: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'other PRs or explicit ordering this PR depends on, e.g. "4160 needs 3908 first"' },
    concerns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'each concern: who raised it (human maintainer vs agent), what, and current status' },
    concernsAddressed: { type: 'boolean', description: 'true if every raised concern has a concrete response (commit or substantive reply) after it' },
    staleRisk: { type: 'string', description: 'is the change still needed/valid vs current upstream/main? cite evidence' },
    evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'key facts with file:line / comment dates backing the category call' },
    nextAction: { type: 'string', description: 'one concrete next step for the orchestrator' },
  },
}

phase('Dossier')
const dossiers = await pipeline(
  PRS,
  n => agent(`Build a state dossier for gastownhall/beads PR #${n} by quad341. You are read-only: use gh and read-only git/grep; do NOT edit, push, comment, or use bd.

Gather:
1. gh pr view ${n} --repo gastownhall/beads --json title,body,state,isDraft,mergeable,mergeStateStatus,reviews,comments,files,statusCheckRollup,headRefName — read the FULL thread including review bodies and comments.
2. Who raised concerns and are they addressed? Distinguish HUMAN maintainers (coffeegoddd = storage owner, steveyegge = project owner) from agent reviews (signed "on behalf of matt wilkie", "beads/reviewer", quad341's own agent posts). A concern is addressed only if a commit or substantive reply landed AFTER it.
3. Stacking: does the body/title reference other PRs or be-* bead ordering (e.g. be-x42v.1 foundation vs .4 tests) implying another PR must merge first? Check whether referenced PRs are merged (gh pr view <ref> --json state,mergedAt).
4. Staleness vs current main: the local clone /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main has upstream/main fetched (read-only!). Check whether the files the PR touches still exist/moved on upstream/main (git -C /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main log --oneline -5 upstream/main -- <path>; grep for key symbols). Flag if the problem the PR solves appears already fixed on main.
5. storageAdjacent: from the file list — internal/storage/**, migrations, schema, dolt internals, or anything influencing store-open/migration-gate behavior.

Categorize (exactly one):
- merge-ready: MERGEABLE, CI green, has at least one substantive review, no unaddressed concerns
- needs-rebase: CONFLICTING is the main blocker (concerns otherwise addressed or none)
- needs-review: MERGEABLE + green but no substantive review yet
- needs-worker: concrete unaddressed concern or red CI needing code/doc work beyond a rebase
- blocked-on-human: an open question only a human maintainer can answer
- close-candidate: obsolete/superseded on main, or premise no longer holds (cite proof)
- wait-ci: checks currently running on a fresh push
If both conflicting AND unaddressed concerns, pick needs-worker and say so in evidence.
Draft PRs: note isDraft in evidence; categorize by underlying state anyway.

Be precise in evidence (dates, file:line, who said what). Your final output is the structured object only.`,
    { label: `dossier:${n}`, phase: 'Dossier', model: 'sonnet', schema: SCHEMA })
)

const ok = dossiers.filter(Boolean)
log(`dossiers complete: ${ok.length}/${PRS.length}; budget spent ~${Math.round(budget.spent()/1000)}k`)
return { dossiers: ok, missing: PRS.filter((n, i) => !dossiers[i]) }

content.text:
The survey is complete, two easy wins are already merged, and the heavy work is dispatched to background agents. Here's the map and the plan of record.

## Survey: quad341's open items (gastownhall/beads; nothing open on mybd)

**Base health:** main is on an active merge train (several pushes in the last hour; each cancels the previous CI run). Preflight re-checks base health before every merge.

**Already resolved this session** (reviewed + green + concerns addressed → preflight, squash-merged):
- **#4161** docs: schema-skew guard README/CHANGELOG — prior review said "merge as-is", docs verified against implementation. Merged.
- **#4213** hermetic domain/fs test suite — prior review found only non-blocking nits; I re-ran the affected test package at PR-head merged onto current main (pass). Merged.

**The remaining 18 PRs:**

| PR | State | CI | Reviews | Disposition |
|----|-------|----|---------|-------------|
| 3632 refuse test DBs on prod dolt (storage) | mergeable | green | none | **cross-vendor review pair running now** (opus + codex, per storage-change policy) |
| 3710 12s slow-path fix | mergeable | green | coffeegoddd repro ask (answered by quad); July triage found PR body claims a "Layer 1" federation.go fix that isn't in the diff | **worker dispatched**: verify mismatch, fix gate-doc/body, confirm bug still live on main |
| 3813 PG wisp resolve (+9) | mergeable | green | coffeegoddd: "need beads-only repro" — still unanswered | **worker dispatched** to build & run the repro |
| 4029 import prefix sync, 4159 wisp-deps test | mergeable | pending | none | fresh pushes today — wait for CI, then review |
| 3458 SearchIssueSummaries perf | conflicting | green | coffeegoddd CHANGES_REQUESTED; quad responded substantively but the storage owner's architectural question is unanswered since May 17 | blocked-on-human; will keep rebased |
| 3906, 3908, 3919, 3920, 3971, 4022, 4055, 4160, 4226, 4345 | conflicting | green | some have agent reviews | **dossier workflow running** — judging staleness, stack order (e.g. 4160 tests likely need 3908 first), and concerns before dispatching rebase workers |
| 3663 (draft) bench purge | conflicting | red (33 fails) | none | dossier will judge; likely needs quad or a worker, lowest priority |
| 4045 (draft) postgres docs | mergeable | green | none | dossier, then a reviewer |

All 20 PRs come from the same fork with maintainer-edit enabled, so workers can push fixes/rebases directly to quad's branches.

**quad341's 5 open issues** (4982, 4960, 4314, 4018, 4016) are queued as the next wave after the PR dossiers return.

**In flight right now (all background):** codex reviewer + opus reviewer on 3632, workers on 3710 and 3813, and a 14-PR dossier workflow. Tracking bead: `mybd-woa8`. As results land I'll adjudicate (verifying reviewer claims at PR head before acting, per the review-pairing memories), dispatch rebase/fix workers in worktrees, and merge what comes back clean — reporting outcomes as they land.

<result>## Report: STOP — Postgres backend no longer exists in beads; a "beads-only minimal repro" is now categorically impossible

**No repro was built, pushed, or posted.** This is a stronger version of the task's second STOP condition — not "the bug doesn't reproduce," but "the entire storage backend the bug is about was deliberately removed from beads five days before I started this task."

### What I found

1. **coffeegoddd's original ask** (2026-05-10, PR review comment): *"We need a beads-only minimal repro of the bug, otherwise this is a bug with your orchestration layer."*
2. **maphew's follow-up** (2026-06-16): asked for the smallest command sequence, `bd version`, storage mode, OS, expected vs actual output.
3. **quad341's only response** (2026-05-19): code-reading verification, no runnable repro — confirmed by the maintainer's own repro-sweep on 2026-06-16.
4. **The PR itself admits no PG test exists on main**: the description says the PG regression test (`TestResolvePartialIDForWispOnPG`, tag `integration_pg`) lives on a *different* PR (#3768) that depends on PG test-fixture plumbing "not yet on main," and was deliberately not cherry-picked into #3813 "because it would not compile here."
5. **Critical discovery**: `internal/storage/postgres/` **does not exist on current `upstream/main`** at all.
   - `git ls-tree -d upstream/main -- internal/storage/postgres` → empty.

Message text (`internal/configfile/backend_messages.go`): *"storage backend \"postgres\" is no longer supported: direct support for general-purpose server databases was rolled back to keep Beads simple and resource-light; the configured postgres database was not opened or modified..."*

cat > /tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad/pr3813-close.md <<'EOF'
## Closing: superseded by the Postgres backend removal (#4857)

Thank you @quad341 for the careful diagnosis and the code-reading verification here — the analysis of the PG fast-path miss (wisps filtered out of `SearchIssues`, `PostgresStore.GetIssue` falling through issues → wisps) was sound for the codebase as it stood.

However, the premise of this fix no longer exists on `main`:

- Direct PostgreSQL (and MySQL) backend support was rolled back in #4857 (commit `0d7cf0022`, merged 2026-07-16): `internal/storage/postgres/` is gone from `main`, and `bd` now fails closed at startup when configured with a removed backend, before any resolver code runs.
- On the remaining backend (Dolt/embedded Dolt), partial-ID resolution already finds wisps in the fast path (`GetIssueInTx` routes through `IsActiveWispInTx`), so the `GetIssue` fallback this PR adds would never execute its intended path — it would be inert defensive code.
- Consequently the beads-only repro @coffeegoddd asked for can no longer be produced against stock beads at HEAD, and shouldn't remain a blocking ask; the honest resolution is to retire the PR rather than chase it.

If server-database backends return someday, the fallback pattern here (resolver falls through to `store.GetIssue` after a search miss) is worth resurrecting — this PR and its thread are the record of why.

Closing as superseded-by-backend-removal. Thanks again for the contribution and the sustained follow-through on this rig.

_claude-code-fable-5 on behalf of matt wilkie_
EOF

content.text:
**PR #3813 is closed** — resolved, but not by merging. The worker hit a decisive discovery: the entire Postgres backend the fix targets was removed from beads upstream in #4857 (merged July 16). I verified the claim myself against upstream/main (`internal/storage/postgres/` is gone, removal commit `0d7cf0022`) before acting.

What that meant for the PR:

- coffeegoddd's blocking ask — a "beads-only minimal repro" — is now categorically impossible: current `bd` fails closed at startup on a postgres backend config, before the resolver code ever runs.
- On Dolt (the only remaining backend), the fast path already resolves wisps, so quad's 9-line fallback would be inert dead code.

So I posted a full explanation crediting quad341's diagnosis, noted the pattern is worth resurrecting if server backends ever return, and closed it as superseded-by-backend-removal. Also saved a `bd remember` note (`beads-postgres-backend-removed`), since this likely cascades: **#4045 (POSTGRES-BACKEND.md operator docs) is now a probable close-candidate too** — the dossier workflow will confirm before I act on it.

Still running in the background: the opus + codex review pair on #3632, the worker on #3710's description/diff mismatch, and the 14-PR dossier workflow. Scoreboard so far: 2 merged (#4161, #4213), 1 closed (#3813), 17 open.

<summary>Agent "Opus review of PR 3632" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Both nits confirmed. Here is my review data for the adjudicator.

---

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The core change is sound, compiles (`go build ./internal/storage/dolt/` clean on PR head), references live `doltserver.ReadPortFile`/`PortFileName` APIs, and merges cleanly against current `upstream/main`. One should-fix design observation and two nits below.

---

### Finding 1 — should-fix (design): `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` disables the hard 3307 guard, not just the false-positive-prone heuristics — reopening the Clown Show #14 hole for the entire test suite

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:150-153` (`isProductionPort` short-circuit) consumed at `:1118` (`applyConfigDefaults`), `:1153` (`New()` panic), `:1681` (`openServerConnection` firewall).
- **Defect:** `isProductionPort` returns `false` whenever `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=="1"`, which suppresses *all three* detection rules including Rule 1 (`cfg.ServerPort == DefaultSQLPort`, the unambiguous 3307 check). All 13 updated `TestMain`s now set `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` process-wide, so for every test package the pre-existing `BEADS_TEST_MODE=1 → force port 1` rewrite and the `New()` prod-port panic are now bypassed.
- **Failure scenario:** The exact Clown Show #12–#18/#14 class this file exists to prevent — a test picks up a `metadata.json`/env that resolves `ServerPort` to 3307 while `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT` is unset. **Pre-PR:** `applyConfigDefaults` forced port 1 (immediate fail-safe) or `New()` panicked. **Post-PR:** with `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` set globally, `isProductionPort` returns false, so no port-1 rewrite, no panic, and the `New()` name-firewall is also opted out (`:1161` gates on the same var) — a `testdb_*` store connects to and pollutes the real production server on 3307. Normal test runs (ephemeral server, correct `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT`) are unaffected; the regression only bites under the misconfiguration the guard was built to catch.
- **How verified:** Traced all four consumers at PR head; confirmed pre-PR merge-base (`d076e634:store.go:1035,1070,1543`) forced port 1 / panicked on 3307 under `BEADS_TEST_MODE` alone; confirmed all 13 TestMains set both env vars (grep cross-check of `BEADS_TEST_MODE` vs `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` setters).
- **Suggested fix:** Narrow the short-circuit so `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` suppresses only Rules 2 and 3 (the `BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT`/`dolt-server.port` heuristics that can false-positive on a dedicated test box), while Rule 1 (`== DefaultSQLPort`) stays unconditional. A legitimate dedicated test server never runs on 3307, so keeping Rule 1 hard costs nothing and preserves the Clown Show #14 fail-safe. Equivalent alternative: gate `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` only on the `New()` name-firewall (`:1161`) and keep `isProductionPort` port-pure. Note the author's doc-comment states the blanket short-circuit is intentional, so this is a design disagreement to adjudicate, not an outright bug — hence should-fix, not blocker.

### Finding 2 — nit: redundant `db.Close()` in the `openServerConnection` firewall

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:1682`.
- **Defect:** The added `_ = db.Close()` is redundant — the `defer func(){ if !connReady { _ = db.Close() } }()` at `:1642-1646` already closes `db` on this return path (`connReady` is still false). `sql.DB.Close()` is idempotent so this is harmless, but it's misleading (implies the defer doesn't cover this path). The merge-base firewall correctly relied on the defer alone.
- **How verified:** Read both blocks; confirmed `connReady` is not set before the firewall return.
- **Suggested fix:** Drop the explicit `_ = db.Close()`.

### Finding 3 — nit: stale doc reference to a non-existent `uniqueBenchDBName`

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:67-68` and `cmd/bd/dolt.go:984-985`.
- **Defect:** Both comments cite `cmd/bd/dolt.go uniqueBenchDBName` as the source of `benchdb_` names; no such function exists. Bench DB names are actually generated in `cmd/bd/template_test.go:182` via `fmt.Sprintf("benchdb_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())`.
- **How verified:** `grep -rn "func uniqueBenchDBName"` → not defined; located the real generator.
- **Suggested fix:** Point the comment at `cmd/bd/template_test.go:newTemplateBenchmarkStore`.

---

### Drift assessment
Effectively none. Merge-base is `d076e634`; `upstream/main` is 19 commits ahead (`4f0df4e6`) but **every** file this PR touches — `internal/storage/dolt/store.go`, `cmd/bd/dolt.go`, `cmd/bd/doctor/*`, `internal/doltserver/doltserver.go`, and all test files — is byte-identical between merge-base and current main (store.go blob unchanged: `cd6ce892`). `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main pr-3632-review` produces zero conflict markers. The `mergeable: CONFLICTING` in the July triage comment no longer applies; a rebase is clean. All guard insertion points still exist on current main.

### Checked and found sound (coverage)
- **TestMain coverage is complete.** Cross-checked every package that sets `BEADS_TEST_MODE` against those setting `BEADS_TEST_SERVER`. The gaps (`internal/doltserver/*`, `cmd/bd/doctor/federation_test.go`, `cmd/bd/cli_coverage_show_test.go`, root `open_nocgo_test.go`) are all either covered by a same-package/no-build-tag TestMain that does set the var, or in a package (`doltserver`) that never opens a dolt store via `New()` with a test DB name. No package that creates test-named DBs is left without the opt-in.
- **No production (non-test) path creates a test-named DB via `New()`.** `doctest_`/`doctortest_` appear only in cleanup/prefix lists; `benchdb_` and `testdb_` are generated only in test/benchmark code (both under TestMains that set the var). The normal project DB name is not test-prefixed, so the `New()` firewall never fires in production.
- **Env is set process-wide (TestMain), not inline** in the 12 harness edits (verified TestMain bodies); the one cross-process case threads it into subprocess `Cmd.Env` as documented.
- **Store-open ordering is correct for this PR:** the refusal logic lives in `New()`/`applyConfigDefaults`, which run at store open — no dependency on the root `PersistentPreRunE` remote-migrate gate, so the known "flags consulted too late" trap does not apply here.
- **`store_unit_test.go` changes are correct:** the added `t.Setenv("BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT","")` isolations and `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` save/restore in `TestApplyConfigDefaults_TestModeBlocksProdPort` are sound; that test correctly exercises the no-opt-in path.
- **`productionPortReasons` edge handling** (`cfg == nil`, `ServerPort <= 0`, sentinel port 1) is correct; strict `=='1'` opt-in (not truthy) is as advertised.

Non-blocking, already contributor-acknowledged: 17% patch coverage — no direct unit tests for Rules 2/3, the `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` short-circuit, or the `New()` firewall error-return path (tracked as a follow-up in the PR notes).

Relevant files: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-review/internal/storage/dolt/store.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-review/cmd/bd/dolt.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-review/internal/storage/dolt/store_unit_test.go`.</result>

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:150-153` (`isProductionPort` short-circuit) consumed at `:1118` (`applyConfigDefaults`), `:1153` (`New()` panic), `:1681` (`openServerConnection` firewall).
- **Defect:** `isProductionPort` returns `false` whenever `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=="1"`, which suppresses *all three* detection rules including Rule 1 (`cfg.ServerPort == DefaultSQLPort`, the unambiguous 3307 check). All 13 updated `TestMain`s now set `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` process-wide, so for every test package the pre-existing `BEADS_TEST_MODE=1 → force port 1` rewrite and the `New()` prod-port panic are now bypassed.
- **Failure scenario:** The exact Clown Show #12–#18/#14 class this file exists to prevent — a test picks up a `metadata.json`/env that resolves `ServerPort` to 3307 while `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT` is unset. **Pre-PR:** `applyConfigDefaults` forced port 1 (immediate fail-safe) or `New()` panicked. **Post-PR:** with `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` set globally, `isProductionPort` returns false, so no port-1 rewrite, no panic, and the `New()` name-firewall is also opted out (`:1161` gates on the same var) — a `testdb_*` store connects to and pollutes the real production server on 3307. Normal test runs (ephemeral server, correct `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT`) are unaffected; the regression only bites under the misconfiguration the guard was built to catch.
- **How verified:** Traced all four consumers at PR head; confirmed pre-PR merge-base (`d076e634:store.go:1035,1070,1543`) forced port 1 / panicked on 3307 under `BEADS_TEST_MODE` alone; confirmed all 13 TestMains set both env vars (grep cross-check of `BEADS_TEST_MODE` vs `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` setters).
- **Suggested fix:** Narrow the short-circuit so `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` suppresses only Rules 2 and 3 (the `BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT`/`dolt-server.port` heuristics that can false-positive on a dedicated test box), while Rule 1 (`== DefaultSQLPort`) stays unconditional. A legitimate dedicated test server never runs on 3307, so keeping Rule 1 hard costs nothing and preserves the Clown Show #14 fail-safe. Equivalent alternative: gate `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` only on the `New()` name-firewall (`:1161`) and keep `isProductionPort` port-pure. Note the author's doc-comment states the blanket short-circuit is intentional, so this is a design disagreement to adjudicate, not an outright bug — hence should-fix, not blocker.

### Finding 2 — nit: redundant `db.Close()` in the `openServerConnection` firewall

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:1682`.
- **Defect:** The added `_ = db.Close()` is redundant — the `defer func(){ if !connReady { _ = db.Close() } }()` at `:1642-1646` already closes `db` on this return path (`connReady` is still false). `sql.DB.Close()` is idempotent so this is harmless, but it's misleading (implies the defer doesn't cover this path). The merge-base firewall correctly relied on the defer alone.
- **How verified:** Read both blocks; confirmed `connReady` is not set before the firewall return.
- **Suggested fix:** Drop the explicit `_ = db.Close()`.

### Finding 3 — nit: stale doc reference to a non-existent `uniqueBenchDBName`

- **Location:** `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:67-68` and `cmd/bd/dolt.go:984-985`.
- **Defect:** Both comments cite `cmd/bd/dolt.go uniqueBenchDBName` as the source of `benchdb_` names; no such function exists. Bench DB names are actually generated in `cmd/bd/template_test.go:182` via `fmt.Sprintf("benchdb_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())`.
- **How verified:** `grep -rn "func uniqueBenchDBName"` → not defined; located the real generator.
- **Suggested fix:** Point the comment at `cmd/bd/template_test.go:newTemplateBenchmarkStore`.

---

### Drift assessment
Effectively none. Merge-base is `d076e634`; `upstream/main` is 19 commits ahead (`4f0df4e6`) but **every** file this PR touches — `internal/storage/dolt/store.go`, `cmd/bd/dolt.go`, `cmd/bd/doctor/*`, `internal/doltserver/doltserver.go`, and all test files — is byte-identical between merge-base and current main (store.go blob unchanged: `cd6ce892`). `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main pr-3632-review` produces zero conflict markers. The `mergeable: CONFLICTING` in the July triage comment no longer applies; a rebase is clean. All guard insertion points still exist on current main.

### Checked and found sound (coverage)
- **TestMain coverage is complete.** Cross-checked every package that sets `BEADS_TEST_MODE` against those setting `BEADS_TEST_SERVER`. The gaps (`internal/doltserver/*`, `cmd/bd/doctor/federation_test.go`, `cmd/bd/cli_coverage_show_test.go`, root `open_nocgo_test.go`) are all either covered by a same-package/no-build-tag TestMain that does set the var, or in a package (`doltserver`) that never opens a dolt store via `New()` with a test DB name. No package that creates test-named DBs is left without the opt-in.
- **No production (non-test) path creates a test-named DB via `New()`.** `doctest_`/`doctortest_` appear only in cleanup/prefix lists; `benchdb_` and `testdb_` are generated only in test/benchmark code (both under TestMains that set the var). The normal project DB name is not test-prefixed, so the `New()` firewall never fires in production.
- **Env is set process-wide (TestMain), not inline** in the 12 harness edits (verified TestMain bodies); the one cross-process case threads it into subprocess `Cmd.Env` as documented.
- **Store-open ordering is correct for this PR:** the refusal logic lives in `New()`/`applyConfigDefaults`, which run at store open — no dependency on the root `PersistentPreRunE` remote-migrate gate, so the known "flags consulted too late" trap does not apply here.
- **`store_unit_test.go` changes are correct:** the added `t.Setenv("BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT","")` isolations and `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` save/restore in `TestApplyConfigDefaults_TestModeBlocksProdPort` are sound; that test correctly exercises the no-opt-in path.
- **`productionPortReasons` edge handling** (`cfg == nil`, `ServerPort <= 0`, sentinel port 1) is correct; strict `=='1'` opt-in (not truthy) is as advertised.

Non-blocking, already contributor-acknowledged: 17% patch coverage — no direct unit tests for Rules 2/3, the `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` short-circuit, or the `New()` firewall error-return path (tracked as a follow-up in the PR notes).

input.command:
cat > /tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad/pr3632-review.md <<'EOF'
## Cross-vendor paired review (claude-opus + codex-gpt-5.6-sol), adjudicated

Thanks @quad341 — the AD-01 firewall is a real safety win and the mechanics are largely sound: the guard runs at store open (`New()`/`applyConfigDefaults`), TestMain coverage of the opt-in is complete across every package that creates test-named DBs, and `productionPortReasons` edge handling is correct. Both reviewers verified the branch now merges cleanly onto current `main` (every touched file is byte-identical between merge-base and main tip), so no rebase is needed.

Both reviewers independently converged on one blocking finding:

### [P1 — both reviewers, independently] `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` disables the *unconditional* 3307 guard, not just the false-positive-prone heuristics

`internal/storage/dolt/store.go:151-153` — `isProductionPort` returns `false` whenever `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1`, suppressing all three detection rules including Rule 1 (`cfg.ServerPort == DefaultSQLPort`, the unambiguous 3307 check). The `New()` name-firewall at `store.go:1161` honors the same opt-out. Since all 13 updated `TestMain`s set `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` process-wide, a test run that inherits a config/env resolving to port 3307 (e.g. stray `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT=3307` or a picked-up `metadata.json`) loses both pre-existing fail-safes: no port-1 rewrite, no `New()` panic — test DBs get created on the real production server. That's the exact incident class this file exists to prevent, reopened for the whole test suite under misconfiguration.

**Suggested fix (either variant):** keep Rule 1 (`== DefaultSQLPort`) unconditional and let `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` suppress only Rules 2–3 (the heuristics that can false-positive on a dedicated test box — a legitimate test server never runs on 3307); or keep `isProductionPort` port-pure and gate the opt-in only on the `New()` name-firewall. We recognize the doc-comment says the blanket short-circuit is deliberate — if there's a use-case for a dedicated test server *on 3307* we're missing, please say so and we'll re-adjudicate.

### Nits (claude-opus)

- `store.go:1682` — the explicit `_ = db.Close()` in the firewall return path is redundant; the `connReady` defer at `:1642-1646` already closes it. Harmless (idempotent) but implies the defer doesn't cover the path.
- `store.go:67-68` and `cmd/bd/dolt.go:984-985` — comments cite `uniqueBenchDBName` which doesn't exist; bench names come from `cmd/bd/template_test.go` (`newTemplateBenchmarkStore`). Point the comments there.

Patch coverage of the new rules/opt-in paths is thin (~17%) but already acknowledged as a tracked follow-up in the PR notes — not blocking.

A worker will follow up with the Rule-1 fix on this branch shortly (maintainer-edit); happy to re-review immediately after.

_claude-code-fable-5 adjudicating claude-opus-4-8 + codex-gpt-5.6-sol-high, on behalf of matt wilkie_
EOF

content.text:
Wave dispatched. The mid-rebase compiler diagnostics popping up are expected — those are builders' worktrees in conflict-resolution state.

**Scoreboard after this round: 2 merged, 5 closed, 13 open (from 20).**

Closed this round, each with verified evidence and a credited close comment:
- **#4226** — the test it adds already exists verbatim on main (landed via quad's own #4152, commit `9fea6d3ba`).
- **#4045** — Postgres operator docs for a backend that #4857 removed entirely.
- **#4055** — FatalError* refactor superseded by coffeegoddd's project-wide HandleError*/RunE conversion (`5d87c3001`).
- **#3908** — superseded by #4160; I verified by patch-id + tree comparison that 4160 carries all of 3908's content (quad's own claim, confirmed), ending the circular "merge the other one first" deadlock.

Reviews: posted the adjudicated **cross-vendor REQUEST_CHANGES on #3632** — both opus and codex independently found the same P1 (the `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` opt-in bypasses even the unconditional port-3307 production guard, reopening the incident class the PR exists to prevent); I spot-verified at PR head before posting. #3710's worker landed its description/diff reconciliation and confirmed the fix still targets a live hang risk on main.

Now running in background (7 tasks):
- Builder fixing #3632's P1 + nits on quad's branch
- Builders rebasing **#4160** (now the canonical max-rows PR), **#4022**, **#3971** (dropping the hunk superseded by #4158), **#4345** (dropping the hunk superseded by #4552), **#3920**
- opus + codex review pair on **#3710**

Still queued after this wave: the design-heavy reworks (#3663 purge fix needs rewriting against main's renamed bench API; #3906 lite-SELECT must be re-homed into #4150's generic dispatch; #3919 needs slimming to just its unique pieces since #3914 landed its core), CI-waiters #4029/#4159, #3458 (genuinely blocked on coffeegoddd's architectural call — I'll keep it rebased but won't override a human CHANGES_REQUESTED), and quad's 5 open issues.

The only conflict was `docs/CONFIG.md`, which upstream deleted in a docs reorg (`5c2fe0d49`, content merged into `docs/reference/configuration.md`, which no longer has a narrative Profiling section for any flag). I resolved this by dropping this PR's `docs/CONFIG.md` hunk (`git rm docs/CONFIG.md`) rather than reintroduce a file upstream removed. `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md` (auto-generated, second commit) still correctly documents `--mem-profile`.

**Result:** `git diff upstream/main...HEAD --stat` = `cmd/bd/main.go | 29 +++` and `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md | 1 +` — same net diff shape as the original PR, minus the now-orphaned doc file. Both original commits (authored by Jim Wordelman) preserved through the rebase; no new commits of my own were needed.

**Validation (worktree `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3920-rebase`):**
- `go build -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/...` — clean
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/` — clean (without the tag, this environment fails on missing system ICU header per the repo's known build-tag requirement, unrelated to this PR)

**PR comment posted:** https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3920#issuecomment-5065569077 — describes the rebase and conflict resolution, and notes the 2026-06-16 repro-recipe comment appears misdirected (this PR only adds a profiling flag), pointing to `bd --mem-profile <file> <cmd>` as the demonstration.
