## Summary

Rebased gastownhall/beads PR #3920 (branch `feat/be-tg2o` on fork `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib`) onto current `upstream/main` (`4f0df4e6a`).

**Conflict encountered (not the STOP condition):** `cmd/bd/main.go` merged cleanly — the flag-registration/`PersistentPostRun` seam is unchanged in shape, so no redesign was needed. The only conflict was `docs/CONFIG.md`, which upstream deleted in a docs reorg (`5c2fe0d49`, content merged into `docs/reference/configuration.md`, which no longer has a narrative Profiling section for any flag). I resolved this by dropping this PR's `docs/CONFIG.md` hunk (`git rm docs/CONFIG.md`) rather than reintroduce a file upstream removed. `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md` (auto-generated, second commit) still correctly documents `--mem-profile`.

**Result:** `git diff upstream/main...HEAD --stat` = `cmd/bd/main.go | 29 +++` and `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md | 1 +` — same net diff shape as the original PR, minus the now-orphaned doc file. Both original commits (authored by Jim Wordelman) preserved through the rebase; no new commits of my own were needed.

**Validation (worktree `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3920-rebase`):**
- `go build -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/...` — clean
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/` — clean (without the tag, this environment fails on missing system ICU header per the repo's known build-tag requirement, unrelated to this PR)
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 -run 'Profile' ./cmd/bd/.` — `ok`, no test currently matches the `Profile` name pattern (PR doesn't add its own test)

**Push:** confirmed remote tip (`2e8615a56b7d28431eb94233979fa89ef0af4abe`) matched the PR's `headRefOid` before force-pushing `HEAD:feat/be-tg2o` to `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib`. `gh pr view` now reports `mergeable: MERGEABLE`, `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` (CI still needs to run), `headRefOid: 372115759aaf5bc09199e4234ad18d57559a26ca`.

**PR comment posted:** https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3920#issuecomment-5065569077 — describes the rebase and conflict resolution, and notes the 2026-06-16 repro-recipe comment appears misdirected (this PR only adds a profiling flag), pointing to `bd --mem-profile <file> <cmd>` as the demonstration. Signed `_claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_` as instructed.

No bd/dolt commands were run. No files outside `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3920-rebase` were touched other than the scratchpad comment file at `/tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad/pr-3920-comment.md`.</result>

Two more results in:

- **#4345**: rebased clean — the builder verified the supersession claim (dropped the prime_test.go commit that #4552 already landed), replayed the two still-valid commits conflict-free, targeted tests pass, PR is now MERGEABLE/CLEAN with CI running. - **Codex on #3710**: three P2 findings — one substantive (the new 2s timeout turns *slow-but-valid* `dolt remote -v` calls into "no remote found", which can break git-protocol sync routing on loaded hosts) and two concrete bugs in the repro script (probe runs in the caller's cwd instead of the constructed server root; `dolt init --quiet` doesn't exist in dolt 2.2.0 so the script dies under `set -e`). I'm holding adjudication until the opus half of the pair reports, then I'll post one merged review and route the fixes.

Still running: builders on 4160, 4022, 3971, and the 3632 P1 fix; opus reviewers on 3710 and 3920. I'll batch-check CI on the rebased PRs (3920, 4345) plus the two CI-waiters (4029, 4159) when the next results land.

<result>I have completed the verification pass. Here is my adjudicator report.

---

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The shipped code (Layer 2 + Layer 3) is correct and safe — no blockers. Layer 2 is genuinely valuable and clean. Layer 3 is correct but its rationale is stale on current `main`. The real problems are all in the repro tooling/docs, which the reconciliation commit did not fully clean up.

---

## Findings (ranked)

### 1. should-fix — repro.sh never `cd`s into the broken server root; the central measurement is invalid
`/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/repro.sh:52-53`

The "Baseline: dolt remote -v against the broken server root (slow)" block times `dolt remote -v` (line 53) **in the script's invocation directory**, not in `$SERVER_ROOT`. There is no `cd "$SERVER_ROOT"` anywhere between the directory's creation (line 37) and the timed call. Contrast line 86, which correctly subshells `(cd "$PROPER_REPO" && dolt remote -v ...)`. 

Failure scenario: run from the beads checkout (not a Dolt repo), the timed call fails fast against the wrong directory, so the slow-path branch (`> 5000ms`) never fires and the repro "demonstrates" nothing about the multi-DB server root. **How verified:** read the full script; grepped every `cd` and `dolt remote -v` occurrence — the slow-path call is unqualified.
Fix: `(cd "$SERVER_ROOT" && dolt remote -v 2>&1) || true`.

Separately, the "PARTIAL FIX … Layer 2 timeout capped it" branch (lines 68-71) is unreachable-by-design: the 2s cap lives in bd's Go `exec.CommandContext`, not in `dolt`, so a raw `dolt remote -v` is never capped. The script is a bug-repro of the underlying dolt slowness, not a pre/post harness for the fix — the labeling implies otherwise.

### 2. should-fix — reconciliation left the repro docs contradicting the corrected gate doc
`.../scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/repro.sh` (header lines 13-22, summary 92-98) and `.../REPRO.md` (lines 8, 20, 45, 68-69, 75, 77)

The reconciliation commit `3272193ed` corrected the gate doc and PR body to state that the "Layer 1 `repo_state.json` sentinel in `internal/storage/dolt/federation.go`" **never shipped** — but both repro files still describe it as a shipped fix ("federation.go now checks for repo_state.json before invoking ListCLIRemotes", "Layer 1 sentinel skips the call", the `migrateServerRootRemotes` table row, and the fictional `syncCLIRemotesToSQL → ListCLIRemotes` call chain). This is exactly the doc-vs-diff inconsistency the reconciliation was meant to remove; it fixed `release-gates/be-1he-slow-path-fix-gate.md` + the PR body but not `scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/`. **How verified:** diffed `8e18581dc..HEAD`, read the maintainer-edit note in the gate doc, then grepped `Layer 1|repo_state|federation.go|syncCLIRemotesToSQL` across both repro files.

### 3. should-fix (comment accuracy) — Layer 3's justifying comment names a nonexistent function and describes a slow path that no longer exists on `main`
`.../cmd/bd/version_tracking.go:195-197`

The comment: *"The writeable path triggers `syncCLIRemotesToSQL`, which can be slow against multi-database server roots."* `syncCLIRemotesToSQL` exists nowhere in the tree (only this comment matches). More substantively, on current `upstream/main` the writeable open's remote-migrate gate goes `initSchema → CheckRemoteMigrateGateWithRemoteCheck(hasPersistedCLIRemote) → HasPersistedRemote → doltutil.PersistedRemotes` — the **fast on-disk JSON probe**, not `ListCLIRemotes`/`dolt remote -v`. So skipping the writeable open no longer avoids a 12s hang; it saves at most an `initSchema` round-trip. Layer 3 is not *wrong*, but the comment overstates its benefit. **How verified:** traced `initSchema` (store.go:1539 → CheckRemoteMigrateGate call at ~1562) → `hasPersistedCLIRemote` (federation.go:197) → `HasPersistedRemote` (uses `PersistedRemotes`, federation.go:~204); confirmed the only `ListCLIRemotes` callers are `doctor/federation.go:127` and `FindCLIRemote` (push/pull/fetch routing via `hasMatchingCLIRemote`), neither on the DB-open path.

### 4. note (drift, no action) — Layer 3's scope narrowed by 617-commit drift
`.../cmd/bd/version_tracking.go` autoMigrateOnVersionBump

Since the PR's merge-base, `upstream/main` added early returns to this function: `if cfg.GetBackend() != BackendDolt { return }` and `if cfg.IsDoltProxiedServerMode() { return }` — both **before** the PR's RO probe. In proxied-server mode the function now returns before ever reaching the writeable open, so Layer 3 only bites in embedded Dolt mode. The "12s on bd commands" framing assumes embedded mode. Merge is clean (see drift assessment). **How verified:** `git diff 8e18581dc upstream/main -- cmd/bd/version_tracking.go`.

---

## Drift assessment

- Merge-base is `8e18581dc`, an ancestor of `upstream/main` but **617 commits behind** current `4f0df4e6a`. (`git diff upstream/main` is 11MB — pure divergence, not PR content.)
- **`internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go` (Layer 2): zero drift** vs current main; applies verbatim.
- **`cmd/bd/version_tracking.go` (Layer 3):** upstream diverged in the same function, but at a distinct earlier location (see finding 4).
- **`git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main <PR-head>`: 0 CONFLICT markers.** The PR merges cleanly into current main despite the drift. Both touched packages build (`go build -tags gms_pure_go ./internal/storage/doltutil/ ./cmd/bd/` — clean). I did not build the *merged* tree, but the Layer 3 hunk is additive at a non-overlapping location, so risk is low.

---

## What I checked and found sound

- **Layer 2 timeout — correct.** `context.WithTimeout(2s)` + `defer cancel()` + `exec.CommandContext`; `CombinedOutput()` reaps the child (no leaked process, `Wait` handled internally). On cap the process is SIGKILLed, `err` is non-nil, and it maps into the existing `"dolt remote -v failed: %s: %w"` return — the same error *shape* both callers already tolerate (`doctor/federation.go:127` logs and continues; `FindCLIRemote` returns `""`). Terminal outcome identical to the old 12s failure, ~6x faster. The 2s cap sits comfortably above the ~130ms healthy-repo latency.
- **Layer 3 RO probe — correct and safe.** It is an equivalent early-return to the existing `if dbVersion == Version { return }` check further down. On `roErr != nil` it falls through to the normal writeable path (safe fallback). `roStore.Close()` is called on every path before the compare — no leak. The ignored errors (`dbVersion, _ := ...`) are benign: an empty version simply declines to skip and falls through.
- **Task Q2 (can it wrongly skip needed work on a legitimate single-db root?): No.** The probe keys off `bd_version` equality, not `repo_state.json` presence (the task's framing reflected the stale Layer-1 description, which never shipped). A single-db root already at the current version legitimately needs no migration; if it's behind, the probe doesn't skip. It also runs *after* the pre-v56 recovery block, so that path isn't bypassed.
- **Gate doc** `release-gates/be-1he-slow-path-fix-gate.md` is now honest and self-consistent — it explicitly flags the 2-of-3-layers reality and that `federation.go` was never touched.

Net: the code is landable. The blocking-quality issues are confined to a demo script and its README (findings 1-2) plus one misleading in-code comment (finding 3) — all doc/tooling, none in shipped bd behavior. I'd ask for those to be corrected (cheaply) before merge rather than request changes on the fix itself.

Relevant paths:
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/cmd/bd/version_tracking.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/repro.sh`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/REPRO.md`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/release-gates/be-1he-slow-path-fix-gate.md`</result>

<result>The rendered help line is byte-identical to the doc addition, including alignment and placement. Everything checks out.

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

A clean, well-scoped feature. It builds (`go build -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/` exit 0), is gofmt-clean, and the doc line matches generator output exactly. No correctness defects or regressions found. Two nits, neither a blocker.

### What I checked and found sound

1. **Heap-profile write correctness** (`cmd/bd/main.go:1476-1491`): Writes in `PersistentPostRunE`, after store close / telemetry shutdown / CPU-profile stop — the right lifecycle point for an "on exit" snapshot. Calls `runtime.GC()` before `pprof.WriteHeapProfile` by default (the correct convention, matching `go test -memprofile`), with `BEADS_MEM_PROFILE_NOGC=1` to skip it for peak capture. File handle is `Close()`d. Verified by reading the full PostRun block and comparing against the CPU-profile teardown just above it.

2. **Interaction with `--profile` CPU flag** (`cmd/bd/main.go:963-974` vs `1467-1491`): No conflict. CPU profile starts in PreRun / stops in PostRun into its own timestamped file; heap profile writes a distinct user-named file afterward. Both can be set simultaneously. On early `os.Exit` paths (error returns at 1718/1723, second-signal at 1608) PostRun is skipped, so neither profile is written — but this is exactly the pre-existing `--profile` limitation, so parity holds. input.command:
S=/tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad
cat > $S/pr3710-review.md <<'EOF'
## Cross-vendor paired review (claude-opus + codex-gpt-5.6-sol), adjudicated

The shipped code is close: Layer 2's timeout mechanics are sound (context+CommandContext, child reaped, no leaked process; 2s sits far above the ~130ms healthy-repo latency), Layer 3's read-only probe is a safe equivalent early-return with correct fallback and no store leak, and `git merge-tree` against current `main` shows zero conflicts despite the 617-commit drift. The repro script and one timeout edge need fixing before merge.

### Should-fix

1. **[codex, confirmed by adjudicator trace] 2s cap turns slow-but-valid remote discovery into "remote absent," which can break sync routing.** `internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go:93-95` — on timeout, `ListCLIRemotes` errors; `FindCLIRemote` (`remotes.go:164`) maps *any* error to `""`; its caller (`remotes.go:192`, ensure-path) then attempts `dolt remote add`, which hard-fails when the remote actually exists. Pre-PR, a valid-but-loaded repo taking 2–12s eventually succeeded; post-PR it's SIGKILLed at 2s and mis-read as absent. Fix options: propagate `context.DeadlineExceeded` distinctly instead of folding it into "absent"; or apply the aggressive cap only when `dbPath` lacks `.dolt/repo_state.json` (the known-broken multi-DB server-root case this PR targets) and keep a generous cap otherwise.
2. **[both reviewers, independently] `repro.sh` never enters the broken server root.** Line ~53 times `dolt remote -v` in the invocation directory, not `$SERVER_ROOT` (contrast the correct subshell at ~86), so the central "slow" measurement never exercises the constructed root. Fix: `(cd "$SERVER_ROOT" && dolt remote -v 2>&1) || true`.
3. **[codex] `dolt init --quiet` doesn't exist in dolt 2.2.x** (`repro.sh` ~84); under `set -e` the script dies before producing its summary. Redirect output instead.
4. **[opus] The repro docs still describe the unshipped "Layer 1."** The reconciliation commit fixed the gate doc and PR body, but `repro.sh` (9 stale refs) and `REPRO.md` (5) still present the `federation.go` sentinel and the `syncCLIRemotesToSQL → ListCLIRemotes` chain as shipped/current. Finish the cleanup there too.

### Nits

- `cmd/bd/version_tracking.go:195-197` — comment cites `syncCLIRemotesToSQL`, which exists nowhere in the tree, and overstates Layer 3's benefit: on current `main` the writeable-open gate goes through `doltutil.PersistedRemotes` (fast on-disk JSON probe), so the probe saves an `initSchema` round-trip, not a 12s hang.
- Drift note, no action: `autoMigrateOnVersionBump` on main now returns early for non-Dolt backends and proxied-server mode, so Layer 3 only bites in embedded mode — fine, just worth knowing.

A worker will follow up on this branch with the fixes; happy to re-review immediately after.

scripts/gh-body-lint $S/pr3710-review.md && gh pr review 3710 --repo gastownhall/beads --request-changes --body-file $S/pr3710-review.md && echo POSTED-3710
echo "=== CI states ==="
for n in 3920 4345 4029 4159; do gh pr view $n --repo gastownhall/beads --json number,mergeable,mergeStateStatus,statusCheckRollup -q '"\(.number): \(.mergeable)/\(.mergeStateStatus) fail=\([.statusCheckRollup[]? | select(.conclusion != null and .conclusion != "SUCCESS" and .conclusion != "SKIPPED" and .conclusion != "NEUTRAL")] | length) pending=\([.statusCheckRollup[]? | select(.status != "COMPLETED")] | length)"'; done

Follow-up round on PR 3710. The cross-vendor review you enabled is now posted (REQUEST_CHANGES, latest review on the PR — read it in full first: gh pr view 3710 --repo gastownhall/beads --json reviews). Implement its four should-fixes plus the comment nit on the same branch (your worktree /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix; fetch/reset to current PR head first and confirm the fork tip hasn't moved since your push of 3272193ed — STOP if it has):

1. internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go ListCLIRemotes 2s cap: apply the aggressive cap ONLY when dbPath lacks .dolt/repo_state.json (the broken multi-DB server-root case), keeping a generous timeout (e.g. 30s) otherwise; OR propagate context.DeadlineExceeded distinctly so FindCLIRemote can distinguish "timed out" from "absent" and its ensure-path caller doesn't blind-add. Pick whichever is smaller and clearly correct given the actual callers (remotes.go:192 ensure-path, store.go:1958 URL match, doctor/federation.go:127); explain your choice in the commit message. Add/adjust a unit test for the chosen behavior if the package has precedent for testing ListCLIRemotes.
2. scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/repro.sh ~53: run the baseline probe inside the constructed root: (cd "$SERVER_ROOT" && dolt remote -v 2>&1) || true. Also fix the misleading "PARTIAL FIX / Layer 2 capped it" branch labeling (the 2s cap lives in bd's Go code, not dolt — a raw dolt call is never capped; relabel as demonstrating the underlying dolt slowness).
3. repro.sh ~84: drop the unsupported `dolt init --quiet` flag (dolt 2.2.x rejects it and set -e kills the script); redirect output instead. Then RUN the script end-to-end once to confirm it completes and actually exercises $SERVER_ROOT.
4. Purge the remaining unshipped-"Layer 1" narrative from repro.sh (9 refs) and REPRO.md (5 refs) — same reconciliation you did for the gate doc: describe only what ships.
5. cmd/bd/version_tracking.go:195-197: fix the comment — no syncCLIRemotesToSQL exists; on current main the writeable-open gate uses doltutil.PersistedRemotes (fast JSON probe), so the RO probe saves an initSchema round-trip, not a 12s hang. Say that.

Preflight: go build -tags gms_pure_go ./... ; go vet + targeted tests for doltutil and cmd/bd; run repro.sh once. Push to the contributor branch as before (append commits, no history rewrite; verify tip first). Post a short signed follow-up comment on the PR summarizing the round (body file + /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/scripts/gh-body-lint, sign _claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_). Same STOP conditions as before. Report raw: changes, test/repro output, links.

Round-2 delta review of gastownhall/beads PR #3632. Round 1 (cross-vendor) found a P1: BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1 short-circuited isProductionPort entirely, disabling even the unconditional port-3307 rule. A builder just pushed fix commit dd8112f3c to the branch; the worktree /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-fix has it checked out (read-only for you; do not edit/push/post).

The fix claims: productionPortReasons now evaluates Rule 1 (ServerPort == DefaultSQLPort) unconditionally, returns early before Rules 2/3 when BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1; isProductionPort lost its env short-circuit; buildTestModeProductionPortPanic's fix-list only suggests BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1 when it would actually help (ServerPort != DefaultSQLPort); New() name-firewall opt-out unchanged; redundant db.Close removed; two stale uniqueBenchDBName comments corrected; new test REDACTED.

Verify at head (git show dd8112f3c, plus read the full current state of internal/storage/dolt/store.go and cmd/bd/dolt.go):
1. Is the P1 actually closed on ALL consumers? Trace every isProductionPort/productionPortReasons call site (applyConfigDefaults rewrite, New() panic, openServerConnection firewall) and confirm a 3307-resolved config is refused even with both BEADS_TEST_MODE=1 and BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1.
2. Did the restructure introduce any new hole (e.g. Rule-1 check reads a different field than the one applyConfigDefaults rewrites; ordering issue where port gets rewritten to sentinel 1 before New()'s check making the name-firewall opt-out the only remaining guard — is that acceptable?).
3. Does any REMAINING message text or doc comment still claim the opt-in disables all detection?
4. Any test that now encodes wrong expectations (asserting the old blanket bypass).
Verify-before-filing: trace each finding to file:line and confirm real behavior difference.
Deliver raw adjudicator data: Verdict (CLEAN-PASS / findings remain), findings with file:line + scenario + verification, and coverage notes.

input.prompt:
Review gastownhall/beads PR #3971 "fix(filters): Dolt label-filter regression fix — LabelPattern + LabelRegex" by quad341, freshly rebased+trimmed onto upstream/main — cross-vendor pair, you are the Claude side. Worktree at /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3971-rebase (read-only; do not edit/push/post). bd-main clone at /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main has upstream/main.

Current diff vs upstream/main: LabelPattern/LabelRegex SQL wiring in internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go + internal/storage/issueops/filters.go (+ tests filters_test.go, list_embedded_test.go, cmd/bd/list_test.go), and a regression-record flip: tests/regression/discovery_test.go TestDiscovery_LabelPatternFilterDeadCode docstring + DISCOVERY.md BUG-28 entry now say "fixed (PR #3971)". The truncation-hint hunk from the original PR was dropped as superseded by #4158.

Verify-before-filing method: trace every finding at head, file:line, confirm behavior differs from main.

Check specifically:
1. SQL construction correctness and injection-safety of the LabelPattern (glob→LIKE) and LabelRegex wiring: how globToLikePattern escapes %, _, backslash; how the regex reaches SQL (REGEXP with bound parameter?); parameter binding vs string interpolation everywhere.
2. Semantics: multiple labels + pattern + regex combined — AND/OR consistency with how plain label filters behave on main; case sensitivity consistency.
3. The noted duplication: globToLikePattern defined verbatim in BOTH issueops/filters.go and sqlbuild/filter.go — pre-existing in the PR, but check the two copies are actually identical and can't drift silently in this diff (if one is dead code, say so).
4. Do the tests actually pin the fixed behavior (would they catch a regression to the dead-code state)?
5. The regression-record flip: is claiming BUG-28 "fixed" accurate given what ships here, and does TestDiscovery_LabelPatternFilterDeadCode as updated genuinely exercise the fixed path on an embedded store?
6. Anything in the rebase resolution that looks off vs main's current cmd/bd/list_output.go and #4158's IsStderrTerminal behavior.
Deliver raw adjudicator data: Verdict (APPROVE / APPROVE-WITH-NITS / REQUEST-CHANGES), ranked findings with file:line + failure scenario + verification, drift notes, sound-coverage list.

input.command:
S=/tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad
cat > $S/pr3920-review.md <<'EOF'
## Review (claude-opus, adjudicated): approve with non-blocking notes

Verified after the rebase onto current `main`:

- Heap profile writes at the right lifecycle point (`PersistentPostRunE`, after store close/telemetry/CPU-profile stop), calls `runtime.GC()` before `pprof.WriteHeapProfile` (matching `go test -memprofile` convention) with `BEADS_MEM_PROFILE_NOGC=1` to skip for peak capture; file handle closed.
- No conflict with `--profile` (distinct files, both can be set); early `os.Exit` paths skip both profiles equally — pre-existing parity, not introduced here.
- Flag wins over `BEADS_MEM_PROFILE` env fallback, as advertised.
- `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md:306` addition is byte-identical to the built binary's rendered help, correctly placed.
- Dropping the `docs/CONFIG.md` hunk in the rebase was right (file removed upstream in the docs reorg).

Non-blocking notes for a possible follow-up (not worth another CI round here):
1. `BEADS_MEM_STATS` and `BEADS_MEM_PROFILE_NOGC` are new behavioral surface documented nowhere — worth a line in an env-var reference.
2. `--mem-profile /bad/path` fails silently (`os.Create` error swallowed) — same parity as `--profile`, but an explicit user-supplied path deserves a stderr warning.

Will merge once CI completes green.

_claude-code-fable-5 adjudicating claude-opus-4-8, on behalf of matt wilkie_
EOF
scripts/gh-body-lint $S/pr3920-review.md && gh pr review 3920 --repo gastownhall/beads --approve --body-file $S/pr3920-review.md && echo POSTED-3920

content.content: POSTED-3920

content.text:
Status update — round 2 is fully in motion:

**#3632** (test-DB firewall): the P1 fix is on quad's branch — Rule 1 (port 3307) is now unconditional; `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` only suppresses the two false-positive-prone heuristics, with a new regression test locking it in. Round-2 delta reviews (opus + codex) are running now.

**#3710** (slow-path fix): adjudicated cross-vendor review posted as REQUEST_CHANGES. My own trace settled the vendor disagreement in codex's favor: the 2s timeout makes `FindCLIRemote` report a slow-but-valid remote as *absent*, and the ensure-path caller then blind-adds and hard-fails. The original 3710 worker has been resumed (same agent, retained context) to implement the four should-fixes plus the stale-comment nit.

**#3971** (label-filter fix): rebase verified the bug is still live on main — the builder even flipped the upstream regression record (BUG-28) from "open" to "fixed by this PR" and confirmed the test fails on main, passes on the branch. Cross-vendor review pair now running (codex chained serially behind the 3632 delta review, per the repo's serial-codex rule).

**#3920** (mem-profile flag): opus review came back APPROVE-WITH-NITS (heap-profile lifecycle, GC convention, and doc line all verified correct; two non-blocking notes). Approval posted — merges when CI finishes.

**#4345**: rebased clean, prior review's instruction honored — merges when CI finishes.

Still working: builders on the two big rebases (#4160 max-rows, #4022 stats), and CI on 3920/4345/4029/4159. After those land I'll dispatch the wave-2 design-heavy reworks (#3663, #3906, #3919) and then quad's 5 open issues.

## Summary

Rebased `gastownhall/beads` PR #4022 (branch `feat/be-nnqp-be-4b63-stats-no-blocked`, fork `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib`) onto current `upstream/main` in worktree `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase`.

**Conflict resolutions:**
- `cmd/bd/status.go` — main had extracted `renderStatus(stats, recentActivity)` as a separate function (with `RunE`/error-return + metrics event wrapper) since the branch was cut. Re-homed the `--no-blocked` flag, F1 fix (`BlockedCountSkipped` from `stats.BlockedIssues == nil`), and F2 fix ("(skipped)" rendering) by adding a `noBlocked bool` parameter to `renderStatus`. The second PR commit (080f5633a, the F1/F2 fix) ended up with an empty diff during rebase since I incorporated its logic directly resolving the first conflict — git auto-dropped that commit, no content lost (verified via diff against original PR head).
- `cmd/bd/status_proxied_server.go` (new file, added by an intervening PR) also calls `renderStatus`; updated its call site to pass `noBlocked=false` since the proxied-server path doesn't support the flag.
- `internal/storage/storage.go`, `internal/storage/dolt/queries.go`, `internal/storage/embeddeddolt/statistics.go`, `internal/types/types.go` — `FastStatisticsStore` interface / `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` / `BlockedIssues int→*int` applied cleanly against current interfaces.
- New `*int` call sites added since branch cut (compile breaks, not conflicts): `internal/storage/domain/db/issue.go` (new third SQL-backend `GetStatistics`), `internal/storage/issueops/statistics.go` (`GetStatisticsInTx`), and tests `internal/storage/domain/db/ready_helpers_test.go`, `ready_helpers_usecase_test.go`, `internal/storage/conformance/audit_search-counts-stats.go` — all updated to the nil-guard pointer pattern used elsewhere in the PR.
- Docs: `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md`/`docs/cli-reference/status.md` merged cleanly; two `website/versioned_docs/**/cli-reference/status.md` files no longer exist on main (Mintlify migration deleted the Docusaurus site) — dropped them (`git rm`) rather than resurrecting.

**Validation:**
```
go build -tags gms_pure_go ./...                                                 clean
go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/... ./internal/storage/...                     clean
go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 -run 'Stats|Blocked' ./cmd/bd/... ./internal/storage/...   ok (all packages)
go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 -run 'TestDomainDB/(TestIssueGetStatistics|TestIssueUseCase_GetStatistics)' ./internal/storage/domain/db/...   ok
go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 -run 'TestConformance' ./internal/storage/dolt/... ./internal/storage/embeddeddolt/...   ok
```
(The `domain/db` and `TestConformance` runs were extra checks beyond the spec's exact command, needed because their stats subtests run through a testify-suite/table-driven entrypoint that the `Stats|Blocked` top-level `-run` regex doesn't match.)

**Push:** confirmed fork branch tip (`66351b2e9`) matched the PR head before force-pushing `HEAD` (`867168205586799b04f43c473272cfcae75744c7`) to `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib:feat/be-nnqp-be-4b63-stats-no-blocked`. PR now reports `mergeable: MERGEABLE` (was `CONFLICTING`).

**Comment posted:** https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4022#issuecomment-5065602920 (linted via `scripts/gh-body-lint`, signed `_claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_`).

No new commits authored (only conflict resolution within the existing rebased commits, original authorship preserved), so no `Agent-Signature` commit trailer was needed. No `bd`/`dolt` commands were run, per instructions.</result>

<result>Verdict: CLEAN-PASS. The Round-1 P1 is closed on every consumer, the restructure introduces no new hole, no stale text remains, and no test encodes the old bypass. Details below.

## P1 closure trace (BEADS_TEST_MODE=1 + BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1, resolved port 3307, test-named DB)

Rule 1 now lives before the opt-in early-return in `productionPortReasons` (`internal/storage/dolt/store.go:132-141`): the `cfg.ServerPort == DefaultSQLPort` append happens at line 132-134, and the `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=="1"` early return at 139-141 only skips Rules 2/3 — the reasons slice already carries Rule 1. `isProductionPort` (171-173) is now a thin `len(...) > 0` wrapper with no independent bypass.

Every call site refuses the 3307 config with both env vars set:

1. `applyConfigDefaults` (`store.go:1136`) — `isProductionPort(cfg)` returns true via Rule 1, so `cfg.ServerPort = 1` (line 1137). Port neutralized to the unroutable sentinel. Rule 1 reads `cfg.ServerPort`, the exact field this block rewrites — no field mismatch.
2. `New()` panic (`store.go:1171`) — reached after (1) already rewrote the port to 1, so `isProductionPort` is now false and no panic fires. This is correct: the port is already 1 (fails loud, cannot reach production). The panic remains live as defense-in-depth for any path that hands `New()` a still-3307 config (Rule 1 unconditional → panic).
3. `openServerConnection` firewall (`store.go:1708`) — `isTestDatabaseName && isProductionPort`; with TEST_SERVER=1 and port 3307, Rule 1 makes `isProductionPort` true, so CREATE DATABASE is REFUSED. This is strictly *tightened* by the fix — pre-fix the whole-function bypass returned false here and allowed the create.

The "sentinel-1 before New()'s check" ordering the task flags is acceptable: the port is neutralized to 1 (cannot route to a production server and fails loudly), so the name-firewall opt-out at `store.go:1179` is *not* the only remaining guard — the production endpoint is already gone before the name check runs. Confirmed by `REDACTED`, which I ran green (`go test -run TestApplyConfigDefaults_TestModeBlocksProdPort ./internal/storage/dolt/` → ok).

## Question 2 (new holes): none

- Rule-1/rewrite field agreement verified above.
- No new suppression path. Rules 2/3 (`BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT`, `dolt-server.port` file) remain suppressible by TEST_SERVER=1 by design — that is the intended "dedicated test-server lane" for operator-declared non-3307 ports, and it is unchanged from pre-fix behavior for those ports. Not the P1.

## Question 3 (stale "disables all detection" text): none

Grep of all non-test `BEADS_TEST_SERVER` references (`store.go:103,110,136,159,166,1176,1186,1219`) shows every doc comment now says "narrows to Rule 1 only" / "Rules 2 and 3 are suppressed" / "never suppressed by BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1". The name-firewall comment/error (`store.go:1176,1186`) correctly describes its own independent opt-out. The `isProductionPort` doc (159-167) explicitly disclaims the old "single env var toggles the entire split" language.

## Question 4 (tests encoding old bypass): none

`grep -rn isProductionPort/productionPortReasons --include=*_test.go` finds references only in `store_unit_test.go`, all updated. The pre-existing `TestApplyConfigDefaults_TestModeBlocksProdPort` covers the no-opt-in path (unsets TEST_SERVER, line 305) and its comment was rewritten to the new semantics. The 11 `testmain`/setup files that `os.Setenv("BEADS_TEST_SERVER","1")` are global test-server setup (to pass the name-firewall on ephemeral ports), not assertions about 3307 — none assert port stays 3307 or `isProductionPort==false`.

## Secondary changes verified

- `buildTestModeProductionPortPanic` fix-list (`store.go:1216-1225`): logic is sound. When the panic fires with `ServerPort != DefaultSQLPort`, the matched reason must be Rule 2/3, which means TEST_SERVER was not set (else those are suppressed and no panic) — so suggesting `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` genuinely would resolve it. When `ServerPort == 3307`, Rule 1 fired and the opt-in cannot help, so it is correctly omitted.
- Removed `_ = db.Close()` at the firewall-refusal return (`store.go:1708`): the refusal returns without setting `connReady = true`, and the `defer func(){ if !connReady { _ = db.Close() } }()` at `store.go:1667-1672` covers it. Removal is safe (avoids a harmless double-close), not a leak.
- benchdb comment correction (`cmd/bd/dolt.go:984-985`, `store.go` list): accurate — `uniqueBenchDBName` exists nowhere in the tree; the real generator is `newTemplateBenchmarkStore` at `cmd/bd/template_test.go:170`, format `benchdb_%d` / `time.Now().UnixNano()` (`template_test.go:182`) = `benchdb_<unixnano>`.

## Coverage note (not a finding)

A latent design tension survives, unchanged and out of scope: with `BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1` and no `BEADS_TEST_MODE`, a test-named DB on an operator-declared `BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT` (non-3307) is still allowed to be created (`store.go:1708` → `isProductionPort` false because Rule 2 is suppressed). This is identical to pre-fix behavior (pre-fix suppressed all rules including 3307; the fix only hardened 3307) and matches the documented opt-in semantics, so it is not a regression from this commit. Worth a tracking bead only if the team wants `BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT` to be non-suppressible like Rule 1.

Files reviewed: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-fix/internal/storage/dolt/store.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-fix/cmd/bd/dolt.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-fix/internal/storage/dolt/store_unit_test.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3632-fix/cmd/bd/template_test.go`.</result>

input.command: bd create --title="Upstream design question: should BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT be non-suppressible like the 3307 rule in beads' test/prod firewall?" --description="Discovered during cross-vendor review of gastownhall/beads PR 3632 (2026-07-23). After the Rule-1 hardening (dd8112f3c), BEADS_TEST_SERVER=1 still suppresses Rule 2 (BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT) and Rule 3 (dolt-server.port file): a test-named DB on an operator-declared non-3307 production port can still be created when the opt-in is set without BEADS_TEST_MODE. Identical to pre-fix behavior and matches documented opt-in semantics, so not a regression - but if operators expect their declared production port to be as hard as 3307, Rule 2 should also be non-suppressible. Raise upstream as a design question if it bites." --type=task --priority=3 --deps discovered-from:mybd-woa8 --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.id // "created"'; for n in 3920 4345 4029 4159; do gh pr view $n --repo gastownhall/beads --json number,mergeable,mergeStateStatus,statusCheckRollup -q '"\(.number): \(.mergeable)/\(.mergeStateStatus) failed=\([.statusCheckRollup[]? | select(.conclusion == "FAILURE" or .conclusion == "CANCELLED" or .conclusion == "TIMED_OUT")] | length) pending=\([.statusCheckRollup[]? | select(.status != "COMPLETED")] | length) total=\(.statusCheckRollup | length)"'; done

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The shipped fix is correct, injection-safe, and semantically consistent with existing label filters. BUG-28 is genuinely fixed and genuinely tested end-to-end. The nits are test-placement / dead-code hygiene and a coverage gap on the regex path — none change shipped behavior.

---

### Verification performed (all at HEAD `5237d9c45`)

- Built + ran `TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelPattern/LabelRegex`, `TestGlobToLikePattern`, `TestListLabelFiltersAcnquj` (embedded Dolt store), `TestEmbeddedList/label_pattern`, and `TestDiscovery_LabelPatternFilterDeadCode` (real `bd` binary + embedded store, tags `regression discovery gms_pure_go`) — all pass.
- Confirmed injection safety: every user value is a bound `?` param; `tables.Labels` comes from the fixed `FilterTables` set (`labels`/`wisp_labels`), and `ESCAPE '|'` is a literal. No string interpolation of user input anywhere in the new hunks.
- Confirmed `whereClauses` are AND-joined (`internal/storage/dolt/iter_issues.go:49`), so `LabelPattern`/`LabelRegex`/`Labels` combine with AND — consistent with how plain `Labels` already behaved on main.
- Confirmed gms supports the `REGEXP` infix operator (`go-mysql-server .../sql/expression/comparison.go`), so `label REGEXP ?` is engine-valid.
- Confirmed rebase cleanliness: final diff touches no `list.go`/`list_output.go`; main's `printTruncationHint` already gates on `ui.IsStderrTerminal()` (#4158) at `cmd/bd/list_output.go:17`, and the PR correctly dropped its own competing json-gate hunk.

---

### Ranked findings

**1. (should-fix, low) The detailed glob-escaping test pins the DEAD copy, not the production copy — false coverage + silent-drift hazard.**
- `internal/storage/issueops/filters.go:25` defines `globToLikePattern`, but `issueops.BuildIssueFilterClauses` merely delegates to `sqlbuild.BuildIssueFilterClauses` (filters.go:48). The only production caller is `internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go:188`, which uses the **sqlbuild** copy (filter.go:325). The issueops copy has zero non-test callers — it is dead in production.
- The two copies are currently byte-identical (verified with `diff`), but `TestGlobToLikePattern` (`internal/storage/issueops/filters_test.go`, `package issueops`) calls the **issueops** (dead) copy. So the escaping edge cases (`%`→`|%`, `_`→`|_`, `|`→`||`) are asserted only against code that never runs. On the live path, only `tech-*`→`tech-%` is pinned (via `TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelPattern`, which routes through the real sqlbuild).
- Failure scenario: someone edits the sqlbuild escaping (e.g. drops the `|`-escape) — `TestGlobToLikePattern` still passes against the untouched issueops copy, and staticcheck U1000 won't flag the dead func because a same-package test references it. Escaping regression ships unseen.
- Fix: delete the issueops copy and its now-unused `strings` import; export `sqlbuild.GlobToLikePattern` (or move `TestGlobToLikePattern` into `internal/storage/sqlbuild`) so the escaping cases exercise the live function. This also deletes more than it adds.
- Note: the backslash question in the brief is a non-issue — choosing `|` as the ESCAPE char (not `\`) makes literal backslashes pass through correctly; the docstring's `%`/`_`/`|` list is accurate.

**2. (should-fix, low) No end-to-end test for `--label-regex` (REGEXP) against a real store.**
- `label REGEXP ?` (`internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go:191`) is the **only** SQL `REGEXP` usage in the entire codebase, and it is asserted only at clause-string level (`TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelRegex` checks the string `"label REGEXP ?"`). No test runs a regex filter against the embedded Dolt store (grep for `label-regex`/`LabelRegex`/`REGEXP` in `list_embedded_test.go` and `discovery_test.go` returns nothing).
- I verified gms supports the operator, so this is a coverage gap, not a known break. But without a store-level test it won't catch a future engine regression, invalid-regex error surfacing, or case-sensitivity surprises. Suggest adding a `--label-regex "tech-(debt|legacy)"` subtest alongside the existing `label_pattern` case in `cmd/bd/list_embedded_test.go` (which already builds a `dolt.DoltStore`).

**3. (nit) Case-sensitivity consistency is unasserted.** LIKE / REGEXP / `label = ?` all follow gms column collation; under the default `ci` collation they align, but nothing pins it. Fine to leave; note it if factory routing later depends on case-exact label matching.

**4. (nit) `release-gates/be-ucslk4-dolt-ship-gate.md` (+28) is a local process artifact shipping in an upstream PR.** Not a correctness issue; upstream maintainers may not want it. Candidate for dropping from the PR before merge.

**5. (nit) Stale commit message on `8796af148`.** Its body still says it gates `printTruncationHint` on `jsonOutput` and adds `TestPrintTruncationHintJSONSuppression`, but the rebase reduced it to test-only (+125 in `list_test.go`, `TestListLabelFiltersAcnquj`). The tree is correct; only the message is misleading. Cosmetic.

---

### Regression-record flip (BUG-28) — accurate

`tests/regression/DISCOVERY.md` and `tests/regression/discovery_test.go` marking BUG-28 "FIXED — PR #3971" is correct. `TestDiscovery_LabelPatternFilterDeadCode` builds the real `bd` binary (`go build -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd`, `tests/regression/regression_test.go:124`) and runs `bd list --label-pattern "tech-*"` against a live embedded store; it passed here. It genuinely catches a regression to the dead-code state: under the old behavior `filter.LabelPattern` was unconsumed → full set returned → `containsID(patternIDs, b)` fires. The docstring/status edits accurately describe what ships. One asymmetry: the flip claims both `LabelPattern` and `LabelRegex` fixed, but this discovery test only exercises the pattern (glob) path — the regex claim rides on finding No.2's untested path.

---

### Sound coverage (verified, no action)

- SQL construction + injection safety: bound params throughout, fixed table names, literal ESCAPE. Clean.
- Glob→LIKE escaping logic: `*`→`%`, `?`→`_`, `%`/`_`/`|`→`|`-escaped, backslash literal — correct given `ESCAPE '|'`.
- AND/OR semantics: `LabelPattern`/`LabelRegex` AND-join, matching plain `Labels`. Consistent.
- Rebase resolution: no drift vs main's `list_output.go`; truncation-hint hunk correctly ceded to #4158's `IsStderrTerminal` gating.
- `count.go` / wisps paths inherit the wiring for free via `BuildIssueFilterClauses` + `WispsFilterTables` (covered by `TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelPatternWispsTable`).

- The two copies are currently byte-identical (verified with `diff`), but `TestGlobToLikePattern` (`internal/storage/issueops/filters_test.go`, `package issueops`) calls the **issueops** (dead) copy. So the escaping edge cases (`%`→`|%`, `_`→`|_`, `|`→`||`) are asserted only against code that never runs. On the live path, only `tech-*`→`tech-%` is pinned (via `TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelPattern`, which routes through the real sqlbuild).
- Failure scenario: someone edits the sqlbuild escaping (e.g. drops the `|`-escape) — `TestGlobToLikePattern` still passes against the untouched issueops copy, and staticcheck U1000 won't flag the dead func because a same-package test references it. Escaping regression ships unseen.
- Fix: delete the issueops copy and its now-unused `strings` import; export `sqlbuild.GlobToLikePattern` (or move `TestGlobToLikePattern` into `internal/storage/sqlbuild`) so the escaping cases exercise the live function. This also deletes more than it adds.
- Note: the backslash question in the brief is a non-issue — choosing `|` as the ESCAPE char (not `\`) makes literal backslashes pass through correctly; the docstring's `%`/`_`/`|` list is accurate.

**2. (should-fix, low) No end-to-end test for `--label-regex` (REGEXP) against a real store.**
- `label REGEXP ?` (`internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go:191`) is the **only** SQL `REGEXP` usage in the entire codebase, and it is asserted only at clause-string level (`TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelRegex` checks the string `"label REGEXP ?"`). No test runs a regex filter against the embedded Dolt store (grep for `label-regex`/`LabelRegex`/`REGEXP` in `list_embedded_test.go` and `discovery_test.go` returns nothing).
- I verified gms supports the operator, so this is a coverage gap, not a known break. But without a store-level test it won't catch a future engine regression, invalid-regex error surfacing, or case-sensitivity surprises. Suggest adding a `--label-regex "tech-(debt|legacy)"` subtest alongside the existing `label_pattern` case in `cmd/bd/list_embedded_test.go` (which already builds a `dolt.DoltStore`).

**3. (nit) Case-sensitivity consistency is unasserted.** LIKE / REGEXP / `label = ?` all follow gms column collation; under the default `ci` collation they align, but nothing pins it. Fine to leave; note it if factory routing later depends on case-exact label matching.

**4. (nit) `release-gates/be-ucslk4-dolt-ship-gate.md` (+28) is a local process artifact shipping in an upstream PR.** Not a correctness issue; upstream maintainers may not want it. Candidate for dropping from the PR before merge.

**5. (nit) Stale commit message on `8796af148`.** Its body still says it gates `printTruncationHint` on `jsonOutput` and adds `TestPrintTruncationHintJSONSuppression`, but the rebase reduced it to test-only (+125 in `list_test.go`, `TestListLabelFiltersAcnquj`). The tree is correct; only the message is misleading. Cosmetic.

---

### Regression-record flip (BUG-28) — accurate

`tests/regression/DISCOVERY.md` and `tests/regression/discovery_test.go` marking BUG-28 "FIXED — PR #3971" is correct. `TestDiscovery_LabelPatternFilterDeadCode` builds the real `bd` binary (`go build -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd`, `tests/regression/regression_test.go:124`) and runs `bd list --label-pattern "tech-*"` against a live embedded store; it passed here. It genuinely catches a regression to the dead-code state: under the old behavior `filter.LabelPattern` was unconsumed → full set returned → `containsID(patternIDs, b)` fires. The docstring/status edits accurately describe what ships. One asymmetry: the flip claims both `LabelPattern` and `LabelRegex` fixed, but this discovery test only exercises the pattern (glob) path — the regex claim rides on finding No.2's untested path.

---

### Sound coverage (verified, no action)

- SQL construction + injection safety: bound params throughout, fixed table names, literal ESCAPE. Clean.
- Glob→LIKE escaping logic: `*`→`%`, `?`→`_`, `%`/`_`/`|`→`|`-escaped, backslash literal — correct given `ESCAPE '|'`.
- AND/OR semantics: `LabelPattern`/`LabelRegex` AND-join, matching plain `Labels`. Consistent.
- Rebase resolution: no drift vs main's `list_output.go`; truncation-hint hunk correctly ceded to #4158's `IsStderrTerminal` gating.
- `count.go` / wisps paths inherit the wiring for free via `BuildIssueFilterClauses` + `WispsFilterTables` (covered by `TestBuildIssueFilterClauses_LabelPatternWispsTable`).

<result>I have completed the verification pass. Build passes with both CGO and non-CGO (`-tags gms_pure_go`); targeted tests green.

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The int→*int migration is complete and internally consistent, the F1/F2 fixes survived the rebase intact, and the interface plumbing is compile-time safe. No blockers or correctness regressions.

## Findings (ranked)

### 1. should-fix — New `--no-blocked` path has zero test coverage
`grep` for `NoBlocked` / `no-blocked` / `BlockedCountSkipped` across all `*_test.go` returns nothing. The entire new surface is untested:
- `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` returning nil `BlockedIssues` (dolt `queries.go:144`, embeddeddolt `statistics.go:43`)
- the `"(skipped)"` render branch (`cmd/bd/status.go:128-144`)
- `blocked_count_skipped:true` JSON emission (`status.go:114`)
- the proxied silent-ignore path

Why it matters: the existing tests were all *updated* to assert the default path stays non-nil (good), but the actual new feature behavior is a coverage hole. A future refactor could make `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` accidentally populate `BlockedIssues` (breaking the "(skipped)" render) with no test failing. Suggested fix: add one table test asserting `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` leaves `BlockedIssues == nil`, and a `renderStatus(..., noBlocked=true)` / JSON test asserting `blocked_count_skipped=true`.

### 2. nit — `ready_issues` JSON is silently wrong (not null) under `--no-blocked`
`GetStatisticsNoBlocked` never sets `ReadyIssues`, so it stays the zero value `0`. Since `ReadyIssues int` (types.go:1267) is not a pointer, `bd stats --no-blocked --json` emits `"ready_issues": 0` alongside `"blocked_issues": null`. Human output correctly shows `(skipped)` for both (status.go:140-143), but a JSON consumer cannot distinguish "0 ready" from "not computed" — only `blocked_count_skipped` hints at it, and that flag is named for *blocked*, not ready. Failure scenario: a CI script reading `.summary.ready_issues` from `--no-blocked --json` treats the rig as having no ready work. Suggested fix: document in the flag help / status.md that `ready_issues` is meaningless when `blocked_count_skipped` is true, or make it `*int` too.

### 3. nit — Proxied-server path silently ignores `--no-blocked`
`cmd/bd/status.go:79` calls `runStatusProxiedServer(rootCtx, showAssigned, noActivity)` — `noBlocked` is dropped. `status_proxied_server.go:34-35` always calls the full `GetStatistics` and hardcodes `renderStatus(..., false)`. Output remains *correct* (full blocked count, no error), but the flag becomes a no-op with no user signal. Documented in the inline comment; acceptable as graceful degradation. Suggested: a one-line stderr note, or mention the limitation in the flag help.

## Sound-coverage list (verified, no issue)

1. **Migration completeness** — every non-test reader of `BlockedIssues` (status.go, examples/library-usage/main.go:117, conformance, all backends) is nil-guarded or is a writer; `go build ./...` passes CGO and non-CGO, so no unconverted `int` reader survives.
2. **Backend nil-vs-zero agreement** — all three full paths (`dolt/queries.go:132`, `embeddeddolt/statistics.go:28`, `domain/db/issue.go:947`, plus `issueops.GetStatisticsInTx:55` and `buildAssignedStats:229`) set `BlockedIssues = &blocked`. Only the `*NoBlocked` variants leave it nil. `nil == skipped`, non-nil (incl. 0) == computed, uniformly. Confirmed by updated tests asserting `NotNil` on the default path (ready_helpers, queries_test, conformance audit).
3. **JSON default-path shape** — main emitted `"blocked_issues": <number>`; the default (no `--no-blocked`) path still always emits a number since the pointer is always populated. No shape regression for existing consumers; `null` only appears under the new opt-in flag. `StatusOutput.BlockedCountSkipped` uses `omitempty`, so default JSON is unchanged there too.
4. **Interface plumbing (finding #3 in your brief)** — no fragile runtime type assertion exists. `FastStatisticsStore` is folded into the `DoltStorage` interface (storage.go:384) and the global `store` is statically typed `storage.DoltStorage` (main.go:49), so `store.GetStatisticsNoBlocked(ctx)` is compiler-enforced. Both concrete Dolt stores implement it; there is no "store doesn't implement it" path to fall back from.
5. **F1 (Blocked:0 not red)** — verified against `upstream/main:cmd/bd/status.go`, which unconditionally used `ui.RenderFail` for Blocked. Head only reddens when `*BlockedIssues > 0` (status.go:130), else plain `%d`. Genuine fix, intact.
6. **F2 ("(skipped)")** — present at status.go:129 (Blocked) and :141 (Ready). Intact.
7. **Rebase fidelity** — the three PR commits (25425a5e3, 58a5b8c02, 867168205) sit cleanly on the current upstream tip (4f0df4e6a); F1/F2 and all nil-guards present; nothing from the original head lost. (Note: the two Docusaurus doc deletions mentioned in your brief are absent from `git diff upstream/main --name-status` — upstream already removed them, so the rebase correctly dropped them; not a loss.)

Relevant files: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/cmd/bd/status.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/cmd/bd/status_proxied_server.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/types/types.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/storage/storage.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/storage/dolt/queries.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/storage/embeddeddolt/statistics.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/storage/issueops/statistics.go`.</result>

<result>VERDICT: CLEAN-PASS — all four round-1 should-fixes and the comment nit are resolved at head 0a2eabbc4, with no new breakage.

Evidence per round-1 item:

**1. Timeout scoping (was should-fix #1) — RESOLVED.**
`internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go:50-55` — `listCLIRemotesTimeout(dbPath)` stats `dbPath/.dolt/repo_state.json`: absent → 2s (`listCLIRemotesTimeoutBroken`, line 33), present → 30s (`listCLIRemotesTimeoutHealthy`, line 44). Wired into the real call at `remotes.go:118`.
- Path correctness: `cmd.Dir = dbPath` (line 121) is the exact dir the stat probes (`filepath.Join(dbPath, ".dolt", "repo_state.json")`) — consistent, no path skew.
- Resolves the failure mode: a slow-but-valid repo always has `repo_state.json` present → gets 30s, so it can no longer be SIGKILLed and misread as "remote absent." The 2s path is confined to exactly the `repo_state.json`-absent case, which is by definition not a valid repo (dolt itself errors on it), so there's never a real remote to misread. 30s vs ~130ms typical loaded latency (230x margin) is clearly above realistic slow-but-valid range.
- TOCTOU/symlink: stat-then-exec race is theoretical only (`repo_state.json` materializing between stat and exec is not a real scenario; the round-1 failure was a persistently-present-but-slow repo → 30s). `os.Stat` follows symlinks, resolving the same path `cmd.Dir` uses — benign. Not worth flagging.
- The residual structural conflation (FindCLIRemote folds *any* error, incl. a 30s timeout, into `""`) is pre-existing, out of scope, and explicitly reasoned about in the commit body. Not a new finding.

**2. New test — genuinely pins selection, binary-independent.**
`remotes_test.go:136-166` — `TestListCLIRemotesTimeout` calls the pure `listCLIRemotesTimeout` helper directly across 3 cases (no `.dolt` → 2s; `.dolt` but no `repo_state.json` → 2s; `repo_state.json` present → 30s), asserting the exact constant each returns. It does not shell out to `dolt`, uses `t.TempDir()`, and passes (`ok ... 0.004s`). It tests the actual 2s-vs-30s branch logic, not "function returns." Minor gap: it exercises the helper in isolation, not its wiring into `context.WithTimeout` — but that wiring is a single unambiguous line (`remotes.go:118`) and testing it for real would require a hung dolt subprocess. Acceptable tradeoff, not a finding.

**3. Narrative purge — verified.**
All surviving `Layer 1 / federation.go / syncCLIRemotesToSQL / migrateServerRootRemotes` hits in `scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/{repro.sh,REPRO.md}` are in explicit "no longer exists on current main" / "never shipped in be-1he" / "an earlier draft described" reconciliation framing (REPRO.md:32-39, 98-102; repro.sh:30-32). No text describes them as current/shipped behavior. The "PARTIAL FIX ... Layer 2 capped it" branch is relabeled to "SLOW PATH PARTIALLY OBSERVED ... raw uncapped dolt call" (repro.sh diff), and the baseline now runs `(cd "$SERVER_ROOT" && dolt remote -v)` with `SERVER_ROOT` defined at line 47 before use. `dolt init --quiet` → `dolt init >/dev/null 2>&1`. `bash -n` passes.

**4. version_tracking.go comment — now accurate.**
`cmd/bd/version_tracking.go:195-200` claims the writeable-open gate reads remotes via `doltutil.PersistedRemotes`. Confirmed: the remote-migrate gate `HasPersistedRemote` (`internal/storage/dolt/federation.go:204-226`) calls `doltutil.PersistedRemotes` (line 214) — a `repo_state.json` file read, no dolt subprocess. The comment no longer cites the nonexistent `syncCLIRemotesToSQL` and correctly reframes the benefit as saving an `initSchema` round-trip, not a 12s hang.

**5. Build/format hygiene — clean.**
`gofmt -l` clean on all three Go files; `go build`/`go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./internal/storage/doltutil/` clean; new imports (`os`, `path/filepath`) are used. No stale references to a removed single-timeout constant anywhere. (The earlier plain `go build` ICU failure is the known CGO/`uregex.h` environment issue, not this PR.)

One nit (commit-message prose only, not shipped code): the commit body claim that `syncCLIRemotesToSQL` "doesn't exist anywhere in the tree" is slightly overstated — it still appears as a conceptual reference in `internal/storage/dolt/remote_migrate_gate_test.go:27,80,134` comments. No function by that name exists, and the shipped code/comments are correct, so this does not affect the fix. Not worth a re-push on its own.

- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/internal/storage/doltutil/remotes_test.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3710-fix/cmd/bd/version_tracking.go`

<result>VERDICT: CLEAN-PASS — all four round-1 should-fixes and the comment nit are resolved at head 0a2eabbc4, with no new breakage.

Evidence per round-1 item:

**1. Timeout scoping (was should-fix #1) — RESOLVED.**
`internal/storage/doltutil/remotes.go:50-55` — `listCLIRemotesTimeout(dbPath)` stats `dbPath/.dolt/repo_state.json`: absent → 2s (`listCLIRemotesTimeoutBroken`, line 33), present → 30s (`listCLIRemotesTimeoutHealthy`, line 44). Wired into the real call at `remotes.go:118`.
- Path correctness: `cmd.Dir = dbPath` (line 121) is the exact dir the stat probes (`filepath.Join(dbPath, ".dolt", "repo_state.json")`) — consistent, no path skew.
- Resolves the failure mode: a slow-but-valid repo always has `repo_state.json` present → gets 30s, so it can no longer be SIGKILLed and misread as "remote absent." The 2s path is confined to exactly the `repo_state.json`-absent case, which is by definition not a valid repo (dolt itself errors on it), so there's never a real remote to misread. 30s vs ~130ms typical loaded latency (230x margin) is clearly above realistic slow-but-valid range.
- TOCTOU/symlink: stat-then-exec race is theoretical only (`repo_state.json` materializing between stat and exec is not a real scenario; the round-1 failure was a persistently-present-but-slow repo → 30s). `os.Stat` follows symlinks, resolving the same path `cmd.Dir` uses — benign. Not worth flagging.
- The residual structural conflation (FindCLIRemote folds *any* error, incl. a 30s timeout, into `""`) is pre-existing, out of scope, and explicitly reasoned about in the commit body. Not a new finding.

**2. New test — genuinely pins selection, binary-independent.**
`remotes_test.go:136-166` — `TestListCLIRemotesTimeout` calls the pure `listCLIRemotesTimeout` helper directly across 3 cases (no `.dolt` → 2s; `.dolt` but no `repo_state.json` → 2s; `repo_state.json` present → 30s), asserting the exact constant each returns. It does not shell out to `dolt`, uses `t.TempDir()`, and passes (`ok ... 0.004s`). It tests the actual 2s-vs-30s branch logic, not "function returns." Minor gap: it exercises the helper in isolation, not its wiring into `context.WithTimeout` — but that wiring is a single unambiguous line (`remotes.go:118`) and testing it for real would require a hung dolt subprocess. Acceptable tradeoff, not a finding.

**3. Narrative purge — verified.**
All surviving `Layer 1 / federation.go / syncCLIRemotesToSQL / migrateServerRootRemotes` hits in `scripts/repro-be-1he-slow-path/{repro.sh,REPRO.md}` are in explicit "no longer exists on current main" / "never shipped in be-1he" / "an earlier draft described" reconciliation framing (REPRO.md:32-39, 98-102; repro.sh:30-32). No text describes them as current/shipped behavior. The "PARTIAL FIX ... Layer 2 capped it" branch is relabeled to "SLOW PATH PARTIALLY OBSERVED ... raw uncapped dolt call" (repro.sh diff), and the baseline now runs `(cd "$SERVER_ROOT" && dolt remote -v)` with `SERVER_ROOT` defined at line 47 before use. `dolt init --quiet` → `dolt init >/dev/null 2>&1`. `bash -n` passes.

**4. version_tracking.go comment — now accurate.**
`cmd/bd/version_tracking.go:195-200` claims the writeable-open gate reads remotes via `doltutil.PersistedRemotes`. Confirmed: the remote-migrate gate `HasPersistedRemote` (`internal/storage/dolt/federation.go:204-226`) calls `doltutil.PersistedRemotes` (line 214) — a `repo_state.json` file read, no dolt subprocess. The comment no longer cites the nonexistent `syncCLIRemotesToSQL` and correctly reframes the benefit as saving an `initSchema` round-trip, not a 12s hang.

**5. Build/format hygiene — clean.**
`gofmt -l` clean on all three Go files; `go build`/`go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./internal/storage/doltutil/` clean; new imports (`os`, `path/filepath`) are used. No stale references to a removed single-timeout constant anywhere. (The earlier plain `go build` ICU failure is the known CGO/`uregex.h` environment issue, not this PR.)

One nit (commit-message prose only, not shipped code): the commit body claim that `syncCLIRemotesToSQL` "doesn't exist anywhere in the tree" is slightly overstated — it still appears as a conceptual reference in `internal/storage/dolt/remote_migrate_gate_test.go:27,80,134` comments. No function by that name exists, and the shipped code/comments are correct, so this does not affect the fix. Not worth a re-push on its own.

One residual noted as a tracked follow-up rather than a blocker: whether operator-declared `BEADS_PRODUCTION_PORT` (Rule 2) should also be non-suppressible like Rule 1 — pre-existing semantics, unchanged by this PR.

dedLabelRegex(t *testing.T) {
+	if os.Getenv("BEADS_TEST_EMBEDDED_DOLT") != "1" {
+		t.Skip("probe")
+	}
+	bd := buildEmbeddedBD(t)
+	dir, _, _ := bdInit(t, bd, "--prefix", "rp")
+	seed := seedTestData(t, bd, dir)
+
+	issues := bdListJSON(t, bd, dir, "--label-regex", `back(end|log)`)
+	if !containsID(issues, seed.openBug) {
+		t.Errorf("backend label did not match regex: %v", listIssueIDs(issues))
+	}
+	if containsID(issues, seed.feature) || containsID(issues, seed.chore) {
+		t.Errorf("nonmatching labels survived regex: %v", listIssueIDs(issues))

## Cross-vendor paired review (claude-opus + codex-gpt-5.6-sol), adjudicated

Verdict: the fix is correct and merge-worthy after one cheap hygiene round.

**Converging positives:** both reviewers verified the LabelPattern/LabelRegex wiring is fully parameterized (bound `?` everywhere, fixed table names, literal `ESCAPE '|'` — injection-safe), AND-composes consistently with plain label filters, and covers both issue and wisp tables. codex additionally ran a live embedded-Dolt `--label-regex` probe end-to-end and it passed, and confirmed the BUG-28 regression flip is genuine (test fails on unfixed main, passes here). No blocking findings from either vendor.

**Should-fix before merge (claude-opus, verified):**
1. `TestGlobToLikePattern` pins a *dead* copy of `globToLikePattern`: the production caller is `internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go:325`; the byte-identical copy in `internal/storage/issueops/filters.go:25` has zero non-test callers. The detailed escaping cases (`%`→`|%`, `_`→`|_`, `|`→`||`) are asserted only against code that never runs — an escaping regression in the sqlbuild copy would ship unseen. Fix: delete the issueops copy and move/point the test at the sqlbuild function.
2. No committed store-level test for `--label-regex` (`label REGEXP ?` is the only REGEXP in the codebase and is pinned only at clause-string level). codex's ad-hoc probe proved the path works today; commit an equivalent subtest beside the existing `label_pattern` case in `cmd/bd/list_embedded_test.go` so it stays proven.
3. `release-gates/be-ucslk4-dolt-ship-gate.md` is a contributor-process artifact that doesn't belong in the upstream tree — drop it from the PR.

Nits (no action needed): case-sensitivity follows column collation uniformly (unasserted but consistent); one rebased commit message describes a hunk the rebase removed — cosmetic.

A worker will push the hygiene round shortly; approval + merge to follow immediately after.

_claude-code-fable-5 adjudicating claude-opus-4-8 + codex-gpt-5.6-sol-high, on behalf of matt wilkie_

content.content:
===== 4022 =====
ject the unsupported flag.

- [P2] Render assigned statistics from the returned count state — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/cmd/bd/status.go:128-129
  With `--assigned --no-blocked`, `getAssignedStatistics` replaces the fast-path result with statistics containing a non-nil blocked count and a computed ready count, but these branches still render both values as `(skipped)`. This contradicts the JSON output, where `blocked_count_skipped` is correctly omitted and the counts are present; base human rendering on `stats.BlockedIssues == nil` rather than the original flag.
The new optional path emits a false ready count in JSON and is silently ignored in proxied-server mode. Combined filtering also produces contradictory human and JSON representations.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Mark ready_issues as skipped instead of returning zero — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/internal/storage/dolt/queries.go:152-153
  When `--no-blocked --json` is used on a database with ready work, this method cannot compute `ReadyIssues` but leaves its non-nullable field at zero. The serialized response therefore reports `"ready_issues": 0`, indistinguishable from a genuine zero and potentially misleading the documented CI consumers; make this field nullable/omitted or add an explicit ready-count-skipped signal.

- [P1] Honor --no-blocked in proxied-server mode — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/cmd/bd/status_proxied_server.go:34-35
  For repositories using proxied-server mode, including non-CGO installations that rely on this mode, the flag is discarded and this hard-coded `false` performs the full statistics query and reports a normal blocked count. Thus `bd status --no-blocked` silently provides neither the promised fast path nor skipped-count signaling; implement the fast repository/use-case path or explicitly reject the unsupported flag.

- [P2] Render assigned statistics from the returned count state — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-4022-rebase/cmd/bd/status.go:128-129

## Cross-vendor paired review (claude-opus + codex-gpt-5.6-sol), adjudicated

The core work is sound and survived the heavy rebase intact — both reviewers verified the int→*int migration is complete across all three backends with uniform nil⇔skipped semantics, the `FastStatisticsStore` plumbing is compile-time-safe (no runtime assertion to fall through), the F1 fix (Blocked:0 no longer renders red) and F2 ("(skipped)") are genuine, and default-path JSON shape is unchanged for existing consumers. Findings below are all in the new opt-in surface.

### Blocking (fix before merge)

1. **[codex P1, opus concurring] `ready_issues` is silently wrong in JSON under `--no-blocked`.** `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` can't compute readiness, so the non-pointer `ReadyIssues` serializes as a genuine-looking `0` next to `"blocked_issues": null` — a CI consumer reading `.summary.ready_issues` concludes the rig has no ready work. Make `ReadyIssues *int` (consistent with what this PR already did to `BlockedIssues`) or add an explicit skipped signal.
2. **[codex P1] Proxied-server mode silently discards the flag.** `cmd/bd/status_proxied_server.go:34-35` hardcodes the full query + `noBlocked=false`, so on proxied rigs (including non-CGO installs that depend on that mode) `--no-blocked` delivers neither the fast path nor the skipped signaling, with no user-visible indication. Either implement the fast path there or reject/warn explicitly.
3. **[codex P2, verified at head] `--assigned --no-blocked` contradicts itself.** `getAssignedStatistics` replaces the fast-path stats with fully-computed ones, but the human renderer branches on the *flag* (`cmd/bd/status.go:128,141`) and prints "(skipped)" for values that were computed — while JSON correctly reports them (`BlockedCountSkipped` derives from `stats.BlockedIssues == nil`). Base the human rendering on the data (`stats.BlockedIssues == nil`), not the flag.
4. **[opus] The new surface has zero test coverage.** No test asserts `GetStatisticsNoBlocked` leaves `BlockedIssues == nil`, the "(skipped)" render branch, or `blocked_count_skipped:true` emission. One table test + one render/JSON test closes it.

Fixing (1) and (3) via pointer-consistency largely collapses into the same change: render and serialize from data-state, not flag-state.

A worker will follow up on the branch; re-review after.

_claude-code-fable-5 adjudicating claude-opus-4-8 + codex-gpt-5.6-sol-high, on behalf of matt wilkie_

"Check doc flags freshness" FAILED with a definitive diagnosis: the canonical doc pipeline builds bd from the release pin (docs/cli-docs.pin → v1.1.0), which predates --mem-profile, so the +1 line this PR added to docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md is flagged stale; CI's own generated patch is "docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md | 1 deletion". CI's own uploaded fix artifact was exactly `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md | 1 deletion(-)`.

1. **Check doc flags freshness** — direct failure from the above (job `89384828568`, log line: `FAIL: docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md is out of sync with live CLI help` → `-      --mem-profile string ...`).
2. **Build Artifacts** (job `89384828648`) — not an independent artifact-build bug. Its "Run policy checks" step (step 5) runs `make ci-pr-policy`, which invokes the identical doc-flags script and fails with the same diagnostic (`make: *** [Makefile:124: ci-pr-policy] Error 1`, exit code 2). 3. **PR Policy (wrapper timing)** (job `89384828580`) — same cause: "Run PR policy wrapper" step also runs `make ci-pr-policy` and fails identically (`<== check doc flags failed after 35s with exit code 1` → `Error 1` → exit 2).

content.text:
Tidy outcome on #3920: all three CI failures shared one root cause — the doc-freshness pipeline regenerates from the *pinned* v1.1.0 binary, so the new flag's doc line read as stale, and the same `make ci-pr-policy` invocation cascaded the failure into Build Artifacts and PR Policy. The builder applied CI's own 1-line patch and pushed; the Nix flake failure was confirmed as unrelated infra (upstream hash mismatch before any repo code runs).

Now it's all waiting: CI re-runs on 3920's new head plus the approved set (3632, 3710, 3971, 4345), the 4022 fix-round builder (mid-edit), and the 4160 review pair. Merges start as soon as the first one settles green.
