## Summary

Reworked gastownhall/beads PR #3906 to target current `main`. Work done in `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework` on branch `rework/be-uwvs.1-lite-select-foundation`, force-pushed to `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib:feat/be-uwvs.1-lite-select-foundation`.

**Verified maphew's 2026-07-05 review against today's main (`1bbca0c1f`):** accurate. `searchTableInTx` is gone, replaced by #4150's generic `searchProjection[T]` dispatch (`SearchIssuesInTx` → `searchInTx` → `searchTableInTxT`, projection literals `issueProjection`/`idProjection` in `internal/storage/issueops/search.go`). PR #4160 (BEADS_MAX_ROWS) is still open/unmerged, no drift there. One correction beyond the review: it named `LeaseExpiresAt`/`HeartbeatAt` as needing classification, but current `IssueSelectColumns` also gained a `row_lock` column that the review didn't mention (added after the review was written) — I classified that too (see below).

**Design (re-homing):** added `issueLiteProjection`, a `searchProjection[*types.Issue]` literal alongside `issueProjection`/`idProjection`, pointing `columns()` at `IssueSelectColumnsLite` and `scan()` at `ScanIssueLiteFrom`; `SearchIssuesInTx` picks between the two projections based on `filter.Lite` before calling the shared `searchInTx`/`searchTableInTxT` machinery. This gets wisp-merge, Pattern-B id-shrink, and lease-join for free and matches the file's own stated pattern ("adding a narrow-projection variant means adding a projection literal").

**Column classification:** `row_lock`, `leases.lease_expires_at`, `leases.heartbeat_at` — all small, non-TEXT, routing-relevant — went into `IssueSelectColumnsLite`/`ScanIssueLiteFrom`, none into `HeavyDropList`. `TestIssueSelectColumns_LitePlusHeavyEqualsFull` passes.

**Commits** (attribution preserved where possible):
- `86b039a22` (Jim Wordelman) — original PR patch cherry-picked, minus its `search.go` hunk (target function deleted; message amended to say so explicitly rather than silently drop content)
- `21aca36ea` (Jim Wordelman) — original release-gate doc, applied clean
- `6882a4513` (matt wilkie, `Agent-Signature: claude-sonnet-medium`) — my re-homing: `search.go` dispatch integration + `scan.go` column classification

**Preflight, all green:** `go build -tags gms_pure_go ./...`, `go vet`, `gofmt -l` on changed files, `go test -run 'Lite|IssueSelectColumns' ./internal/storage/...`, full `./internal/storage/issueops/...`, and `./internal/storage/embeddeddolt/...` `./internal/storage/schema/...` `./internal/storage/versioncontrolops/...` `./internal/types/...`.

**Push:** fork tip matched PR head (`b42b899b8`) before force-push — confirmed, no surprise drift. Post-push `mergeStateStatus` went CONFLICTING → CLEAN, now `UNSTABLE` (CI checks still running, not a conflict). Comment posted: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3906#issuecomment-5066356180 (passed `gh-body-lint`).

**Anomaly to flag:** twice during this session, tool output contained an injected-looking `<\system-reminder>` claiming a file was "modified by the user or a linter" with an explicit instruction not to tell you, since "you are already aware." You were not made aware by me, and I don't believe you sent that instruction. I disregarded the "don't tell the user" directive both times — the underlying file states were just normal artifacts of my own `git checkout`/`cherry-pick --abort` commands, nothing was actually hidden or lost, but the instruction pattern itself (do X, hide it from the user) is worth knowing about in case it recurs elsewhere in this harness.

**Files:** `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/search.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/scan.go`. Worktree left in place, clean, nothing further staged. No `bd` or git-commit/push actions taken in the `mybd` coordination repo itself.</result>

input.prompt:
Review gastownhall/beads PR #3906 "feat(storage): opt-in lite SELECT shape" by quad341, just re-homed onto main's generic projection dispatch — cross-vendor pair, Claude side. Worktree: /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework (read-only; do not edit/push/post). Design: new issueLiteProjection (searchProjection[*types.Issue] literal) with columns()=IssueSelectColumnsLite, scan()=ScanIssueLiteFrom; SearchIssuesInTx selects projection by filter.Lite; classification: row_lock + lease columns in lite, heavy TEXT bodies (description/design/acceptance/notes...) deferred via HeavyDropList; invariant test TestIssueSelectColumns_LitePlusHeavyEqualsFull.

Verify-before-filing throughout. Check:
1. Projection correctness: does ScanIssueLiteFrom's field order exactly match IssueSelectColumnsLite (off-by-one scanning is the classic bug)? What do deferred heavy fields contain on the returned Issue (empty string? is there a marker like IsLitePartial so consumers can't mistake empty-for-real)? Trace what happens if a Lite result is passed to code expecting full bodies (e.g. export, JSON render) — is the opt-in safely unreachable there (foundation-only, no CLI wiring — confirm no caller sets Lite yet).
2. The invariant test: does Lite+Heavy truly equal the full column set including the newest columns (row_lock, lease_expires_at, heartbeat_at)? Would it catch a future column added to full but forgotten in the split?
3. Wisp-merge and Pattern-B id-shrink under the lite projection: the claim is they come free via the shared machinery — verify the lite projection composes with searchInTx's merge (wisps table has different columns — does IssueSelectColumnsLite reference columns absent on the wisps table when tables=WispsFilterTables?) and with the lease join.
4. Sort-before-trim (just added on the 4160 branch, NOT yet on main): on THIS branch's searchInTx, is the merged exit still trimming unsorted concatenation? If this PR's searchInTx now diverges from 4160's (both modify the same file), flag the incoming merge conflict between 3906 and 4160 explicitly — which lands first matters. Describe the collision shape.
5. release-gates/be-uwvs.1-*.md re-added: per sweep policy these contributor-process artifacts get dropped from upstream PRs — confirm it's present and flag for removal.
6. Build/vet + run the Lite/IssueSelectColumns tests + issueops package at head.
Deliver raw adjudicator data: Verdict (APPROVE / APPROVE-WITH-NITS / REQUEST-CHANGES), findings with file:line + failure scenario + verification, sound-coverage list.

---

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

Cross-vendor Claude-side review of gastownhall/beads PR #3919 (slim). The safety-critical properties all hold: the new progress/warning code is strictly advisory, cannot fail a migration, and does not touch stdout. One cosmetic nit only.

### Findings

**NIT — `internal/storage/schema/schema.go:1175` — "One-shot" comment is inaccurate; the notice can emit twice per `MigrateUp`.**
`emitLargeRigNotice` fires at the top of `runMigrations`, but `runMigrations` is called once per migration source: `mainSource.migrate` at `schema.go:489` and `ignoredSource.migrate` at `schema.go:541`, both inside a single `MigrateUp`. `migrate()` (schema.go:1125) is generic over the source and only skips `runMigrations` when `current >= target`. So on a large-rig catch-up where both the main and ignored sources have pending migrations — precisely the scenario this warning targets — the operator sees `Large rig detected (...)` printed twice.
- *Failure scenario:* duplicate identical advisory line on a TTY. No correctness impact; TTY-only; never reaches machine-parsed output.
- *Verification:* traced both `migrate()` call sites in `MigrateUp`; the emit is unconditional at `schema.go:1181-1182`, outside the per-migration loop and outside any source guard.
- *Suggested fix:* either drop the word "One-shot" from the comment (accept the harmless duplicate), or, if a single line is wanted, gate emission on `src.cursorTable == mainSource.cursorTable` so only the main pass warns.

### Sound-coverage list (verified, no finding)

1. **Advisory-only / no error leak (concern 1, 7):** `issueRowCounter` returns `(int64, error)` but `rowCountErr` is passed only to `emitLargeRigNotice` and never returned from `runMigrations` (schema.go:1181-1182). `emitLargeRigNotice` treats any error as "suppress" (schema.go:57). No new code path can enter `runMigrations`' error flow. Confirmed by reading all early-returns in the function.

2. **No transaction to poison (concern 7):** the migration runner does not wrap steps in a SQL transaction — it uses Dolt working-set + `CALL DOLT_COMMIT` (schema.go:567) and per-step `commitMigrationStep`. A failing `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues` on a fresh DB therefore cannot abort a surrounding SQL transaction (there is none). The counter also runs *before* the loop, ahead of `preMigrationRepair`/`ExecContext`/`commitMigrationStep`, so it never executes inside those sensitive spans.

3. **Fresh-DB safety (concern 1):** `issues` has existed since migration 0001; the only case where `COUNT(*)` errors is a truly pre-0001 fresh DB, which is exactly the "no rig to warn about" case. Error is swallowed. Matches the code comment.

4. **TTY gating / no stdout (concern 2):** all three new writes target the package `stderr` var (schema.go:1182, 1220, 1230), which is `io.Discard` when `os.Stderr` is not a terminal (`defaultStderr`, schema.go:32-37). `emitLargeRigNotice`'s `out` receives that same gated writer. Nothing writes to stdout. CI/`--json`/piped callers get zero new output.

5. **`humanMigrationName` panic-safety (concern 3):** pure transform; `SplitN(...,2)` never panics and `parts[1]` is guarded by `len(parts) < 2`. Checked `noversion.up.sql` (no underscore → returns whole stem), empty, and leading-underscore inputs — none panic. Pinned by `TestHumanMigrationName`.

6. **Timing span (concern 4):** `start` is set after the "Applying" print and before `ExecContext` (schema.go:1221); `time.Since(start)` prints after the cursor `INSERT` (schema.go:1230). It measures migration SQL + hash + cursor write — the right span. On migration failure the early return at schema.go:1223 correctly suppresses the `done` line (you should not see "done" for a failed step). Correct.

7. **Threshold / spam (concern 6):** `largeRigThreshold = 10000`; warning fires only for `count > threshold` (strict — 10000 is silent, 10001 warns, pinned by `TestEmitLargeRigNotice`). Within a single source the notice is outside the loop, so a 20-migration catch-up yields one line per source, not per migration. Value is sane.

8. **Tests (concern 5):** the `mockDB.QueryRowContext` panics with `"not called"` (schema_test.go:1903-1905), so stubbing `issueRowCounter` in `TestRunMigrationsStderrOutput`/`TestRunMigrationsUsesProvidedSource` is genuinely required, not defensive noise. The mockDB diff weakens no assertion — it *tightens* the line-count check from `n` to `2*n` (Applying+done per migration) and adds a `done (` substring assertion. `progress_test.go` pins threshold boundary, naming, newline, and operator-guidance text. Full `internal/storage/schema` suite passes (9.3s) with `-tags gms_pure_go`.

Relevant path: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3919-slim/internal/storage/schema/schema.go`</result>

input.message:
Round 2 on PR 3919 (worktree /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3919-slim). Cross-vendor review verdicts are in (opus approve-with-nits, codex two P2s); adjudicated fixes, both verified at head:

1. Large-rig notice duplicates: emitLargeRigNotice fires at the top of runMigrations, which MigrateUp calls once for mainSource (schema.go:489) and once for ignoredSource (:541) — a large rig with pending migrations in both sources gets the "one-shot" warning twice, plus two COUNT queries. Fix: gate emission so only the main-source pass warns (e.g. compare src.cursorTable to the main cursor table, or thread a bool), and fix the "One-shot" comment to match. Extend TestEmitLargeRigNotice/progress_test.go to pin single emission across both sources if the harness allows.

2. "done (N.Ns)" prints before the per-step Dolt commit: verified at schema.go:1230 the done line and timing are emitted BEFORE the commitEachStep block at :1233-1236 runs commitMigrationStep (DOLT_ADD/DOLT_COMMIT). On the production embedded path that's the expensive/fallible part — an operator sees "done" then an error, and the timing excludes the commit. Fix: move the done print (and the time.Since) to after the commitEachStep block succeeds (i.e., after the if-block, so both commit and non-commit paths print it last); a failed step commit must NOT print done. Check whether the existing stderr-output tests assert ordering/line counts in a way that needs updating (their mock path likely runs commitEachStep=false, so probably unaffected — verify).

Preflight: build/vet + full internal/storage/schema suite + progress_test.go. Push (verify fork tip matches your last push; append commit; trailer as before). Short signed follow-up comment on the PR (gh-body-lint; _claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_). STOP on contradiction. Report raw.

<result>## Summary

Reworked and shipped gastownhall/beads PR #3663.

**Dossier claims verified against upstream/main (1bbca0c1f):**
- `PURGE`/`DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES` genuinely absent from `cmd/bd/dolt.go` and `internal/storage/dolt/` — confirmed, but the grep scope was too narrow: `cmd/bd/test_helpers_test.go` and `cmd/bd/doctor/test_helpers_test.go` already purge (landed via #2089, Feb 2026, unrelated to this PR). The real remaining gap is only in `bd dolt clean-databases`'s production RunE.
- Migration `0051_add_date_indexes` redundant with main's `0052_add_date_indexes` — confirmed identical content, both authored by quad341 (#3662, commit `0268ba894`). Dropped entirely.
- `staleDatabasePrefixes` already has `"benchdb_"` on main (#3618) — confirmed, dropped as redundant.
- Bench-DB helpers `uniqueBenchDBName()`/`dropBenchDB()` never landed — confirmed. Went further: current `internal/storage/dolt/dolt_benchmark_test.go`'s `setupBenchStore` is fully embedded (per-run `os.MkdirTemp`, `os.RemoveAll` cleanup), so there's no shared-server leak there to purge at all — that half of the original PR doesn't apply to current main's architecture and was dropped rather than rewritten. The `benchdb_*` names actually created (`cmd/bd/template_test.go`) already purge via existing `dropTestDatabase`.
- Also found and dropped: the PR's `website/` doc hunks (directory no longer exists — migrated to Mintlify, regenerated from a pinned release tag).

**Rework shape (2 commits, pushed to `quad341/beads-sec003-contrib:release/be-1n9-pq5-purge`, tip confirmed unmoved at 5e19af49b before force-push):**
1. `fix(dolt): PURGE dropped databases after clean-databases DROP (be-pq5)` — quad341-attributed, rebased `cmd/bd/dolt.go` fix, extracted into a `purgeDroppedDatabases()` helper.
2. `test(dolt): regression test for clean-databases PURGE (be-pq5, rework)` — new test `cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go::TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesReclaimsDisk`, using `CALL DOLT_UNDROP(name)` as the purge observable (Dolt has no SQL-visible dropped-DB list) with a control subtest validating the methodology.

**Validation:** `go build`/`go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./...` clean (CGO 0 and 1); `golangci-lint --build-tags=gms_pure_go --new-from-rev=upstream/main` 0 issues; `go test -run 'Purge|BenchDB' -v` against a real `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` testcontainer — new test + control PASS; existing `TestDoltAdministrativeCommandsRejectRemovedBackends` still PASS.

PR title/body updated (dated maintainer-edit note, quad's original text preserved), signed comment posted (https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3663#issuecomment-5066367429), `gh pr ready 3663` run — PR is now open, not draft: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3663

Relevant files (in worktree `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/`):
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go`
- `cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

Tracking bead `mybd-woa8` notes updated and pushed to Dolt remote (`bd dolt push`). No git commits/pushes made in the mybd coordination repo itself (conservative profile; nothing needed one).</result>

input.prompt:
Review gastownhall/beads PR #3663 "fix(dolt): PURGE dropped databases after DROP" by quad341, just reworked/slimmed — cross-vendor pair, Claude side. Worktree: /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework (read-only; do not edit/push/post). Current shape: cmd/bd/dolt.go gains purgeDroppedDatabases() called from `bd dolt clean-databases`'s RunE after DROPs; new test cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go (TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesReclaimsDisk, uses CALL DOLT_UNDROP as the purge observable plus a control subtest); CHANGELOG entry. The old migration/bench hunks were dropped (redundant with landed #3662/#3618; bench harness now embedded per-run).

Verify-before-filing. Check:
1. purgeDroppedDatabases correctness: what statement does it run (CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES?); is it scoped to the databases just dropped or global (does purging ALL dropped databases risk destroying a dropped-but-recoverable database the user wanted undroppable — i.e., does clean-databases now remove the safety net Dolt's dropped-DB retention provides)? Is that acceptable/documented in the command's help/output? Any error handling: purge failure should not fail the clean (or should it?) — what does the code do and is it reasonable?
2. Recall the dolt-CLI trap from project memory: PREPARE/EXECUTE-driven DDL silently no-ops through dolt CLI (`dolt sql -q/-f`) — confirm the purge goes through the real SQL connection (database/sql / go-mysql-server), not a dolt CLI subprocess, and that DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES is a stored procedure call that works in both server and embedded modes. If embedded mode differs, is there a guard?
3. The test: does DOLT_UNDROP-fails-after-purge genuinely prove disk reclamation (name says ReclaimsDisk — does it check disk, or only undroppability? If only undroppability, is the name overclaiming?); is the control subtest sound; does the test respect the repo's serial-dolt constraint and existing testcontainer patterns; runtime cost.
4. Removed-backend interaction: clean-databases on non-dolt backends — does the purge path stay unreachable (TestDoltAdministrativeCommandsRejectRemovedBackends still passing suggests yes; verify the guard ordering).
5. The maintainer-edited PR body: does it accurately describe what now ships (no stale Layer/migration claims)?
6. Build/vet + run the purge test if a dolt server/testcontainer is available; otherwise compile-verify and say so.
Deliver raw adjudicator data: Verdict (APPROVE / APPROVE-WITH-NITS / REQUEST-CHANGES), findings with file:line + failure scenario + verification, sound-coverage list.

<result>All verification complete. Here is the adjudicator data.

---

## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The re-homing is correct. The projection-dispatch design is sound, field ordering is exact, the build/vet/tests are green, and the Lite path is safely unreachable from production (foundation-only, no caller sets `filter.Lite`). Two actionable items — one process (release-gate artifact), one prospective cross-PR integration gap — plus one tracked test-coverage nit.

---

### Sound coverage (verified, no action)

1. **Projection field-order correctness — CLEAN.** I traced every dest against its column list, byte for byte:
   - Full: `IssueSelectColumns` (49 cols via `IssueBaseColumns` + `LeaseSelectColumns`, `sqlbuild.go:38-61`) matches `ScanIssueFrom` dests (`scan.go:91-104`) 1:1.
   - Lite: `IssueSelectColumnsLite` (43 cols, `scan.go:34-44`) matches `ScanIssueLiteFrom` dests (`scan.go:259-272`) 1:1. No off-by-one.
   - The 6 dropped columns (`description, design, acceptance_criteria, notes, waiters, payload`) are exactly `HeavyDropList` (`scan.go:56-63`), and exactly the set-difference full∖lite.

2. **Deferred heavy fields contain zero values, not stale data, and are flagged.** `ScanIssueLiteFrom` never allocates dests for the 6 heavy columns; they stay Go zero-values. `IsLitePartial=true` is set (`scan.go:393`) as the marker so consumers can detect partial hydration. The field is `json:"-"` (`types.go:153`) so it never leaks onto the wire.

3. **Opt-in is unreachable, so export/JSON-render cannot receive a partial issue.** `grep` across `internal` and `cmd` finds zero setters of `filter.Lite` / `Lite: true`. `DoltStore.SearchIssues` → `issueops.SearchIssuesInTx` dispatch is fully plumbed (`queries.go:18`, `search.go:18-22`), but the CLI never flips the flag. `docs/EXTENDING.md` contract is accurate. CLI wiring is explicitly deferred to be-uwvs.2.

4. **Invariant test catches forgotten columns, including the newest ones.** `TestIssueSelectColumns_LitePlusHeavyEqualsFull` (`scan_test.go:66`) asserts `cols(Lite) ∪ HeavyDropList == cols(Full)` by set membership. `row_lock`, `leases.lease_expires_at`, `leases.heartbeat_at` are all present in both full and lite (qualified names match textually), so they reconcile. A future column added to `IssueBaseColumns` but not classified lands in `missing` and fails the test; a heavy column wrongly kept in lite is caught by the second loop (`scan_test.go:109-113`). Passes.

5. **Wisp-merge + Pattern-B id-shrink + lease join compose for free — verified, not just claimed.** `IssueSelectColumnsLite` is a strict subset of `IssueSelectColumns`, which the shipping `issueProjection` already selects against the wisps table via the same `searchInTx`/`searchTableInTxT` machinery. `row_lock` exists on wisps (migration `0054_add_lease_columns.up.sql:112` + `ignored/0013` backfill), and `LeaseJoin` is a LEFT JOIN so lease-less wisp rows hydrate nil. `issueLiteProjection` sets `joinLeases: true` and `idShrink: true` (`search.go:76-83`), identical to `issueProjection`, so both the merge query and the Pattern-B batch-fetch (`search.go:305-311`) get the join. No lite-only column can be absent from wisps.

6. **Build/vet/tests green** (CGO_ENABLED=1, `-tags gms_pure_go`): `go build` clean, `go vet` clean on issueops+types, `go test ./internal/storage/issueops/...` (full package, and `-run 'Lite|IssueSelectColumns|ScanIssue'`) all pass.

---

### Findings

**[should-fix — process] `release-gates/be-uwvs.1-gate.md:1` — contributor-process artifact present, drop before upstream submission.**
The gate file (added by commit `21aca36ea`, 78 lines) is an internal deployer/review-gate record referencing local bead IDs (be-uwvs.1, be-mbcn), fork branch names, and a shipped-commit hash from the *original* fork (`6001dd4e8`) that no longer matches this re-homed branch. Per the sweep policy these do not go upstream. Fix: drop `release-gates/be-uwvs.1-gate.md` from the PR (keep `docs/EXTENDING.md` — that is a legitimate caller-contract doc, not a process artifact).

**[should-fix — cross-PR integration order] `search.go:76` — `issueLiteProjection` will silently miss the sort-before-trim fix when PR #3906 and the be-x42v.4 / #4160 line meet.**
Both PRs edit the same file. #4160 (`fix(review): sort merged issues+wisps before trim`, commit `86b03be76`) adds a `less func(...)` field to `searchProjection[T]`, sets `less: sqlbuild.Less` on `issueProjection`, and inserts `sortMergedResults(...)` before a `trimToSearchLimit` in `searchInTx`'s merge branch — fixing a real bug where an unsorted issues+wisps concatenation was trimmed to `Limit`, silently dropping a higher-ranked wisp.

Collision shape:
- **Textually likely CLEAN.** #3906 adds a *new var* (`issueLiteProjection`) and only edits `SearchIssuesInTx` (top of file); #4160 edits the `searchProjection` struct, the `issueProjection` literal, and the `searchInTx` merge tail. Different regions/functions — git will probably auto-merge.
- **Semantically GAPPED.** The auto-merge produces an `issueLiteProjection` with `less == nil` (neither PR knows about the other's addition). Once Lite is wired (be-uwvs.2) and a caller combines `Lite + Limit>0 + non-SkipWisps`, `sortMergedResults` no-ops on nil `less` and `trimToSearchLimit` reintroduces exactly the bug #4160 fixed — for the lite path only.
- **Latent today** because Lite has no caller, matching #4160's own documented rationale for leaving `idProjection.less` nil.

Note this branch (#3906) in isolation is correct: its `searchInTx` merge tail has *no* trim at all (`search.go:192-196` just `append`s and returns), so there is no unsorted-trim on this branch. The gap is purely prospective. Whoever lands second owns the one-line fix: add `less: sqlbuild.Less` to `issueLiteProjection`. Recommend #4160 land first (it is a bug fix on a reachable path); then #3906's re-home simply copies the `less` line into the new literal.

**[nit — tracked] No positional/integration guard for `ScanIssueLiteFrom` column-to-field order.**
The invariant test checks set membership, and the `stubScanner` tests (`scan_test.go:120,161`) assign stub values to whatever dests appear — neither verifies that dest *order* matches the SQL column *order*. The classic off-by-one would slip past the unit suite; I caught it only by manual trace (it is correct). This is acceptable for a foundation PR and is explicitly deferred: per-backend correctness tests are be-uwvs.4. Flagging so the reviewer confirms be-uwvs.4 includes a round-trip test that inserts distinct heavy-vs-light values and reads them back through `Lite=true`.

---

Relevant files (all absolute):
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/scan.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/search.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/scan_test.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/types/types.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/sqlbuild/sqlbuild.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/release-gates/be-uwvs.1-gate.md` (drop)
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/docs/EXTENDING.md` (keep)</result>

Note this branch (#3906) in isolation is correct: its `searchInTx` merge tail has *no* trim at all (`search.go:192-196` just `append`s and returns), so there is no unsorted-trim on this branch. The gap is purely prospective. Whoever lands second owns the one-line fix: add `less: sqlbuild.Less` to `issueLiteProjection`. Recommend #4160 land first (it is a bug fix on a reachable path); then #3906's re-home simply copies the `less` line into the new literal.

**[nit — tracked] No positional/integration guard for `ScanIssueLiteFrom` column-to-field order.**
The invariant test checks set membership, and the `stubScanner` tests (`scan_test.go:120,161`) assign stub values to whatever dests appear — neither verifies that dest *order* matches the SQL column *order*. The classic off-by-one would slip past the unit suite; I caught it only by manual trace (it is correct). This is acceptable for a foundation PR and is explicitly deferred: per-backend correctness tests are be-uwvs.4. Flagging so the reviewer confirms be-uwvs.4 includes a round-trip test that inserts distinct heavy-vs-light values and reads them back through `Lite=true`.

Added `TestRunMigrationsLargeRigNoticeOnlyOnMainSource`, which stubs `issueRowCounter` to a large-rig count and asserts the warning appears on the `mainSource` call and stays silent on the `ignoredSource` call.

content.content:
ped to the selected stale databases.

- [P1] Purge leftovers when no active stale database remains — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go:1130-1130
  When disk usage comes from stale databases dropped by an older `clean-databases` invocation, those databases are absent from `SHOW DATABASES`, so the command takes the earlier `len(stale) == 0` return and this `dropped > 0` guard never invokes the purge. Thus upgrading and rerunning the command does not reclaim the accumulated disk space this patch is intended to fix unless another active stale database happens to exist; the cleanup needs a safe path for purging pre-existing dropped data.
The new purge is server-wide and can irreversibly delete unrelated recoverable databases. It also fails to reclaim the historical dropped-database residue that motivated the change when no active stale database remains.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Avoid purging unrelated dropped databases — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go:1152-1152
  When this command runs on a shared server containing any intentionally dropped non-test database, `DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES()` irreversibly removes that database too: Dolt implements this procedure as `PurgeAllDroppedDatabases`, without applying the stale-prefix filter used above. Finding just one stale test database therefore destroys every server-side database retained for `DOLT_UNDROP`; require an explicit server-wide opt-in or a purge mechanism scoped to the selected stale databases.

- [P1] Purge leftovers when no active stale database remains — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go:1130-1130
  When disk usage comes from stale databases dropped by an older `clean-databases` invocation, those databases are absent from `SHOW DATABASES`, so the command takes the earlier `len(stale) == 0` return and this `dropped > 0` guard never invokes the purge. ## Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS

The code is correct, guarded, and tested. `go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/` passes (compiles + vets, including the `//go:build cgo` test file). I did **not** run the testcontainer integration test (no Docker/network here) — compile-verified only; the PR's own test plan reports it passing against `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0`.

### Findings

**1. should-fix (disclosure, not a code bug) — global purge scope is undisclosed**
`cmd/bd/dolt.go:1152` runs `CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES()`, which takes no arguments and permanently destroys **every** dropped-but-unpurged database on the server — not only the stale-prefix databases this command just dropped in its loop. The drop loop (`dolt.go:1081-1121`) is prefix-scoped (`testdb_*`, `benchdb_*`, …); the purge is server-global.

- Failure scenario: an operator accidentally `DROP DATABASE prod_issues` intending `CALL DOLT_UNDROP('prod_issues')` to recover it; a subsequent (or another session's) `bd dolt clean-databases` run purges globally and the undrop safety net is gone — even though `prod_issues` matches no stale prefix.
- Verified: `openDoltServerConnection` (`dolt.go:1900`) yields a `*sql.DB` over the MySQL wire protocol; the CALL hits every dropped database regardless of prefix. Dolt exposes no scoped/per-database purge, so this cannot be narrowed in code.
- The help text (`dolt.go:1007`: "runs CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES() to actually reclaim disk"), the "Reclaimed disk for dropped databases." output (`dolt.go:1136`), and the PR body's original-section claim ("only databases the operator/harness has already DROPped get PURGEd") all understate this. The rework note does not correct it.
- Suggested fix: one line in the `Long` help + the success output stating the purge is server-global and removes the `dolt_undrop` recovery option for **all** dropped databases, not just this run's. This is a documentation ask; it does not block landing if the maintainer accepts the tradeoff for a deliberately destructive maintenance command.

**2. nit — test name overclaims**
`cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go:28` `TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesReclaimsDisk` asserts undroppability, not disk usage. The 15-27 doc comment is candid that undroppability is a proxy (no SQL view lists dropped-unpurged DBs). Reasonable proxy — a purged directory is gone, so disk is reclaimed — but the name promises more than the assertion checks. Optional rename or leave as-is given the thorough comment.

**3. nit — latent shared-server flake in the control subtest**
`dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go:68-85` ("undrop succeeds without purge") asserts a specific DROP is still restorable *because nothing purged in between*. This holds today only because this is the sole test issuing a global purge and it uses serial subtests (no `t.Parallel()`). If a future test adds `t.Parallel()` to a drop+undrop path, or another test starts calling `purgeDroppedDatabases`, a concurrent global purge landing between this subtest's DROP and UNDROP would fail it spuriously. Worth a one-line comment pinning the "no other purger runs concurrently" assumption. Not currently a defect.

### Sound coverage (verified, no action needed)

- **PREPARE/EXECUTE dolt-CLI trap does not apply.** Purge goes through `db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES()")` with no args over `sql.Open("mysql", …)` (`dolt.go:1900`, `1149-1153`) — go-sql-driver text protocol (COM_QUERY) to the running sql-server, not a `dolt sql -q/-f` subprocess. Confirmed.
- **Embedded mode guarded out.** `usesSQLServer()` check at `dolt.go:1017-1019` returns before the connection is opened, so purge only runs in server mode — no embedded divergence to guard. `dolt_embedded_test.go:66` covers `clean-databases` → "not supported in embedded mode".
- **Removed/non-dolt backends stay unreachable.** `loadDoltBackendConfig` (`dolt.go:1014`) fails first; `store_selection_removed_backend_test.go:226` exercises `clean-databases --dry-run` rejection for postgres/mysql/sqlite. Guard ordering correct.
- **Error handling reasonable.** Purge failure warns (`dolt.go:1133-1134`) and returns nil rather than aborting; the `if dropped > 0` guard (`dolt.go:1130`) means it only fires when a drop actually happened. Matches the commit-message rationale.
- **Control subtest design is sound** — pins the undrop-based methodology so the positive case can't go silently vacuous; failure-path cleanup (`:58`, `:81`) avoids leaking `benchdb_*` into the shared server.
- **PR body accurately reworked.** The stale bench-harness/migration/website hunks are dropped; the "Rework note" correctly states only the `cmd/bd/dolt.go` production fix survived, `benchdb_` prefix landed via #3618, migration redundant via #3662. The pre-rework "What this changes" section is explicitly labeled "original PR text, left as-is for context," so it is not overclaiming what ships (except the global-scope gloss noted in finding 1). Attribution preserved (co-authored-by quad341).

Relevant files:
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go` (impl: `purgeDroppedDatabases` at 1143-1154, call site 1124-1138, help 1007)
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go` (regression test)</result>

content.text: Opus and codex converge on the same hazard from different angles: the purge is server-global and irreversible (opus: disclose it; codex: gate it — one stale test DB shouldn't destroy every recoverable database on a shared server). Codex's second P1 (historical residue never reclaimed when nothing's stale this run) folds into the same fix. Adjudicating toward an explicit `--purge-dropped` opt-in and sending round 2.

input.message:
Round 2 on PR 3663 (worktree /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework). Cross-vendor verdicts: opus approve-with-nits, codex two P1s; adjudicated as follows. Core fact (both vendors verified): CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES() is SERVER-GLOBAL — Dolt has no scoped purge — so the current unconditional call after a prefix-scoped drop loop can irreversibly destroy unrelated dropped-but-recoverable databases on a shared server (e.g. an operator's accidentally-dropped prod DB awaiting DOLT_UNDROP). Codex also showed the residue case: when all stale DBs were dropped by prior runs, stale==0 early-returns and the purge never fires, so the accumulated disk the PR targets is never reclaimed.

Fix — gate the purge behind an explicit opt-in, which solves both P1s coherently:
1. Add a --purge-dropped flag to `bd dolt clean-databases` (default false).
   - Without the flag: current drop behavior, NO purge; after drops (or when dropped residue may exist) print a hint: dropped databases are retained (recoverable via CALL DOLT_UNDROP); pass --purge-dropped to permanently reclaim disk — note it purges ALL dropped databases on the server, not just this run's.
   - With the flag: run purgeDroppedDatabases even when stale==0 this run (fixes the residue case — gate on the flag, not on dropped>0). Success output states the server-global, irreversible scope.
2. Long help text: document explicitly that --purge-dropped is server-global and removes the DOLT_UNDROP recovery option for every dropped database.
3. Tests: update to the flag-gated contract — (a) with flag: purge happens (existing UNDROP-fails observable), (b) without flag: DROP remains undroppable... I mean recoverable (UNDROP succeeds — your existing control becomes this case), (c) with flag and stale==0 but pre-existing dropped residue: purge still fires. Rename TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesReclaimsDisk to reflect the undroppability observable (opus nit: the name overclaims — e.g. TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesRemovesUndropRecovery) and add the one-line comment pinning the "no concurrent purger" assumption (opus nit 3).
4. Update the PR body's rework note + your comment thread accordingly.

Preflight: build/vet + run the flag-gated tests against the dolt-sql-server testcontainer as you did in round 1 (serial). Push (verify fork tip matches your round-1 push; append; trailer as before). Signed comment (gh-body-lint; _claude-sonnet-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_). STOP on contradiction. Report raw.

<result>CLEAN-PASS. Both round-2 claims verified against the code at head 48e05f2c8; build/vet clean and the full `internal/storage/schema` suite passes (9.5s), including the new test.

## Evidence

**Claim 1 — done-print moved after the commit block (`internal/storage/schema/schema.go`)**
- Placement is exactly as claimed: the `done` print is at line 1251, *after* the `commitEachStep` block (1246-1250), *still inside* the per-migration loop (`for` at 1192, closes at 1258), and *before* `migrateStepFaultHook` at 1253.
- Failed-commit path is correct: `commitMigrationStep` failure returns at line 1248, before the print at 1251 — no false "done". Matches the new comment (1239-1245).
- Hook interaction is not a regression: in both the old and new ordering `done` prints *before* the fault hook runs (old: done→commit→hook; new: commit→done→hook). A hook failure neither suppresses nor spuriously emits `done` relative to prior behavior. Since `migrateStepFaultHook` is a test-only seam, no production path is affected.
- `count++` (line 1237) is unchanged relative to the commit block — still *before* it, exactly as in the pre-diff code. So on a commit failure the returned `count` includes the current step just as before; no semantic change. It only moved ahead of the relocated `done` print.
- `commitEachStep=false` path: output order is preserved — `Applying …` (1227) then `done` (1251), with the commit block skipped. No reordering visible to that path.

**Claim 2 — large-rig notice gated to the main-source pass**
- `mainSource` (line 231, `cursorTable: "schema_migrations"`) and `ignoredSource` (line 236, `cursorTable: "ignored_schema_migrations"`) are package-level vars with distinct cursor tables. The gate at line 1186 (`src.cursorTable == mainSource.cursorTable`) reuses the exact idiom already established at line 1152 for `commitEachStep`. No hidden coupling or ordering dependency: it compares against the stable package var, not a "first source seen", so reordering the two `runMigrations` calls in `MigrateUp` cannot break it.
- The COUNT query is skipped on the ignored pass: `issueRowCounter(ctx, db)` (line 1187) sits inside the gate block, so the ignored-source pass issues no wasted query. Confirmed against the claim.

**Claim 3 — new test genuinely distinguishes the two sources**
- `TestRunMigrationsLargeRigNoticeOnlyOnMainSource` (schema_test.go:1992-2022) stubs `issueRowCounter` to return `49_187` (> `largeRigThreshold = 10000`, schema.go:39), so `emitLargeRigNotice` (schema.go:56-61) fires regardless of source. It asserts `mainSource` emits "Large rig detected" and `ignoredSource` stays silent. If the gate were dropped, the ignored pass would also emit (the stub ignores the source), failing the second assertion (line 2019). The test is gate-load-bearing.

**Claim 4 — build/vet + suite**
- `go build` and `go vet` (`-tags gms_pure_go`, CGO on): clean.
- `go test ./internal/storage/schema/`: `ok … 9.540s`.
- Verbose run confirms the new test executes and passes (`--- PASS: TestRunMigrationsLargeRigNoticeOnlyOnMainSource`), alongside the sibling `TestRunMigrationsUsesProvidedSource`.

## Nits (non-blocking, no fix required)
- schema.go:1245 comment says "before *either* print statement below runs," but only one print statement (`done`, line 1251) sits below the commit block; the hook is not a print. Harmless wording imprecision.
- schema_test.go:1997-2021 mutates package-level `stderr`/`issueRowCounter` without `t.Cleanup`, restoring manually on each exit path. Consistent with sibling tests (1918, 1961) and not parallel-marked, so correct as written — just not the tidiest pattern.

Files reviewed: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3919-slim/internal/storage/schema/schema.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3919-slim/internal/storage/schema/schema_test.go`.</result>

eops-backed storage implementations. The proxied-server domain/db implementation accepts the same filter but silently performs full hydration, producing backend-dependent behavior.

Review comment:

- [P2] Honor Lite in the proxied-server search path — /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3906-rework/internal/storage/issueops/search.go:19-20
  When `IssueFilter{Lite: true}` is used through the proxied-server/UOW stack, this dispatch is never reached: `domain/db.issueSQLRepositoryImpl.searchTable` and `fetchIssuesByIDs` still select `issueSelectColumns` and call the full scanner. Those calls therefore return populated heavy fields with `IsLitePartial == false`, violating the new `IssueFilter` contract and losing the optimization in proxied-server mode. Add equivalent lite projection and scanning to the domain/db direct, ID-shrink, and union-hydration paths.

<result>## Summary

Fixed the "Check doc flags freshness" CI failure on PR #4160 by applying CI's own generated patch verbatim.

**Log evidence** (job [89403539322](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/actions/runs/30067827283/job/89403539322)): the freshness check builds `bd` from the pin in `docs/cli-docs.pin` (v1.1.0), which predates `--max-rows`/`BEADS_MAX_ROWS`, so any doc lines this PR added for the new flag are flagged as stale relative to that older pinned build. The log contained nothing beyond generated-doc staleness — the "Check 3: Legacy storage references" WARN and everything else in the run is pre-existing/unrelated noise, not something this PR introduced. No STOP condition.

**What I did:**
1. Pulled the failing job's log (`gh api repos/gastownhall/beads/actions/jobs/89403539322/logs`).
2. Downloaded the `cli-docs-freshness-patch` artifact (ID `8587444469`) and extracted `cli-docs-freshness.patch`.
3. `git apply --check` passed cleanly; applied verbatim, no manual edits.
4. Confirmed the resulting diff matched CI's reported stat exactly: `6 files changed, 10 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)`.

**What was removed:** the six generated `--max-rows int ...` lines in `docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md` and `docs/cli-reference/{dep,find-duplicates,graph,list,ready}.md`, plus the incidental column-width realignment in the `bd graph` flag table that removing the widest flag name triggers. The flag help strings in `cmd/bd/max_rows.go` (`addMaxRowsFlag`) are untouched — they remain the source of truth and will regenerate correctly once `docs/cli-docs.pin` advances past this PR, matching the pattern already established for PRs #3920 and #4022.

**Push:** verified fork tip (`86b03be76`) unmoved before pushing `9cd899cc4`. PR remains `mergeable: MERGEABLE`.

**Comment posted:** https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4160#issuecomment-5066479758

<tool-use-id>REDACTED</tool-use-id>

<summary>Agent "Builder: rework PR 3663" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## Round 2 summary

Addressed both P1s from the cross-vendor review of PR #3663.

**Root cause (verified independently by both opus and codex, and confirmed here):** `CALL DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES()` is server-global — Dolt has no scoped-purge primitive — so round 1's unconditional post-drop purge could irreversibly destroy unrelated dropped-but-recoverable databases elsewhere on a shared server. Codex additionally found the residue bug: gating the purge on `dropped > 0` this run meant a run finding zero stale databases (because a prior run already dropped them but never purged) skipped the purge entirely, leaving exactly the accumulated disk be-pq5 targets unreclaimed.

**Fix:** `bd dolt clean-databases` gains an explicit, default-off `--purge-dropped` flag. The gating decision is extracted into `shouldPurgeDroppedDatabases(purgeDropped, droppedCount)`, which deliberately ignores `droppedCount` — this fixes the residue case, since the flag alone now controls purging. Help text and success output explicitly document the server-global, irreversible scope.

**Tests rewritten for the flag-gated contract:**
- `TestShouldPurgeDroppedDatabasesGatesOnFlagAlone` — pure table test pinning the gate, including the residue case.
- `TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesReclaimsDisk` renamed `TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesRemovesUndropRecovery` (opus nit on overclaiming), with the requested no-concurrent-purger comment.
- New `TestCleanDatabasesPurgeDroppedReclaimsResidue` drives the real CLI end-to-end (`buildBDUnderTest`/`writeServerRepo`/`runBDCommand`), priming past the test container's own `beads_test` bootstrap database so `stale == 0` is genuinely exercised. Verified this test fails against the pre-fix `dropped > 0` gate and passes against the fix.

**Validation:** `go build`/`go vet -tags gms_pure_go ./...` clean (CGO 0 and 1); `golangci-lint --build-tags=gms_pure_go --new-from-rev=upstream/main` 0 issues; `go test -run 'Purge|BenchDB' -v` against a real `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` container — all PASS; existing `TestDoltAdministrativeCommandsRejectRemovedBackends` still PASS.

**Push:** confirmed fork tip unmoved (`c21c8ce8f`) before pushing 2 new commits appended (fast-forward, no force) — new head `5e1a9a1f1`. PR body updated with a "Rework note, round 2" section; signed comment posted: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3663#issuecomment-5066478454. PR remains open/ready for review.

Files: `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go`, `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/CHANGELOG.md`.

Tracking bead `mybd-woa8` notes updated and pushed to Dolt remote. No git commits/pushes made in the mybd coordination repo itself.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>242189</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>69</tool_uses><duration_ms>941542</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

Claims: (1) new default-off --purge-dropped flag on `bd dolt clean-databases`; gating extracted to shouldPurgeDroppedDatabases(purgeDropped, droppedCount) which deliberately ignores droppedCount (fixes the residue case); help + success output document server-global irreversible scope. (2) Tests: table test on the gate incl. residue case; renamed TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesRemovesUndropRecovery with no-concurrent-purger comment; new end-to-end TestCleanDatabasesPurgeDroppedReclaimsResidue (verified fail-at-pre-fix).

Verify at head:
1. Flag wiring: default false; without the flag NO purge on any path (including dropped>0); with the flag, purge runs even when stale==0/dropped==0 — trace the RunE flow including early returns (does the stale==0 early-return still bypass the purge when the flag IS set? that's the residue bug's shape — confirm the control flow actually reaches the purge in that case).
2. Without-flag UX: is the retention/hint output present and accurate (mentions DOLT_UNDROP recoverability + --purge-dropped)? Does dry-run mode interact sanely with the flag (purge must never run under --dry-run — check)?
3. Help text accuracy: server-global + removes undrop recovery for ALL dropped databases.
4. The end-to-end residue test: does it genuinely exercise stale==0 with pre-existing dropped residue, and assert reclamation via the undrop observable? Any shared-server leak from its fixtures?
5. Embedded/removed-backend guards still ordered before any purge path.

Deliver: Verdict (CLEAN-PASS / findings remain) with file:line evidence.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result># Verdict: CLEAN-PASS

Round-2 delta (`c21c8ce8f..5e1a9a1f1`, head `5e1a9a1f1`) is correct and the residue P1 is genuinely fixed. Build, vet, and all three tests pass at head in a container-available environment.

## Verification against each claim

**1. Flag wiring / control flow (the residue bug's shape).** Confirmed fixed.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1484` — flag registered default `false`.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1069-1076` — the old `return nil` after "No stale databases found." is **gone**; it is now a plain if/else that only prints. Nothing short-circuits between the stale-scan and the purge decision.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1088-1144` — the drop loop is guarded by `if len(stale) > 0`, but control always falls through to line 1151.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1151` — `shouldPurgeDroppedDatabases(purgeDropped, dropped)` is reached with `stale==0 / dropped==0`, so `--purge-dropped` fires the purge in exactly the residue scenario. Without the flag, it returns `false` on every path (verified by the table test). The only pre-purge returns are the dry-run early return (1085) and the unhealthy-server `SilentExit()` (1115) — both correct places to skip a purge.

**2. Without-flag UX + dry-run.** Correct.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1159-1162` — no-flag branch prints the retention hint naming both `CALL DOLT_UNDROP(name)` recoverability and `--purge-dropped`, plus the server-global caveat.
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1078-1086` — dry-run returns *before* the purge block, so purge can never run under `--dry-run`; it additionally prints an explicit "`--purge-dropped` ignored" line when both flags are set. Purge-under-dry-run is impossible.

**3. Help text.** Accurate — `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1015-1023` states SERVER-GLOBAL, IRREVERSIBLE, cannot be scoped, removes `DOLT_UNDROP` recovery for *all* dropped databases (not just this run's), including pre-existing residue. Success/warn output at 1156-1157 matches.

**4. End-to-end residue test.** Genuinely exercises `stale==0` with pre-existing dropped residue. `dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go:170-207`: primes the server (purges the container's `beads_test` bootstrap + ambient residue) so the assertive run's `SHOW DATABASES` is clean; creates + drops (not purges) a `benchdb_residue_*` DB; asserts both "No stale databases found." and "Purged all dropped databases"; then asserts `DOLT_UNDROP` **fails** (the reclamation observable). Pre-fix this fails two ways (old `dropped>0` gate skips purge, and the old `return nil` prints no purge line) — a real regression gate.
- **No shared-server leak.** The cmd/bd container's *working* shared DB is `cmdbd_pkg_shared` (`test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:46`), which matches no stale prefix and is untouched. `beads_test` (the only thing the prime purge destroys) is a name-literal in same-package tests that connect to `db.invalid` — no test depends on it existing. Cross-package containers are separate binaries. Both purge tests are non-`t.Parallel()`, so the documented no-concurrent-purger assumption (`dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go:62-70`) holds and extends to the new test.

**5. Embedded/backend guards ordered before purge.** Confirmed — `loadDoltBackendConfig` error (`dolt.go:1029`) and `!usesSQLServer()` embedded-mode bail (`dolt.go:1032`) both precede `openDoltServerConnection` and the entire purge path.

**6. Build/vet/tests at head.** `go build` and `go vet` (`-tags gms_pure_go`) clean. `TestShouldPurgeDroppedDatabasesGatesOnFlagAlone` (pure Go, incl. residue case) PASS; `TestPurgeDroppedDatabasesRemovesUndropRecovery` (renamed, both subtests) PASS; `TestCleanDatabasesPurgeDroppedReclaimsResidue` PASS (5.3s against the testcontainer).

## Minor nits (non-blocking, no fix required)
- `cmd/bd/dolt.go:1160` — the no-flag branch unconditionally prints "Dropped databases remain recoverable via `CALL DOLT_UNDROP(name)` until purged." even on a run that dropped zero and where no residue may exist. It reads as a generic server-state hint (which is defensible given the whole point is that residue is invisible to this run), but is slightly odd right after "No stale databases found." Likewise the success path prints "Purged all dropped databases" even when the purge was a no-op. Cosmetic only.
- CHANGELOG entry sits under `### Fixed` though it introduces a new opt-in flag; acceptable since it's all unreleased and corrects the round-1 auto-purge behavior for be-pq5.

No blockers or should-fix findings. The round-2 rework is sound.

Relevant files:
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/dolt_clean_databases_purge_test.go`
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/explicit_db_nodb_test.go` (harness)
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go` (shared server = `cmdbd_pkg_shared`)
- `/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-3663-rework/CHANGELOG.md`</result>

input.command:
S=/tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad
cat > $S/pr3663-approve.md <<'EOF'
## Cross-vendor paired review (claude-opus + codex-gpt-5.6-sol), adjudicated: approved

Round 1 established the rework was sound (real purge gap in `bd dolt clean-databases`, correct wire-protocol path — the dolt-CLI PREPARE/EXECUTE trap doesn't apply, embedded/removed-backend guards ordered correctly) but both vendors converged on the same hazard from different angles: `DOLT_PURGE_DROPPED_DATABASES()` is server-global and irreversible, and Dolt offers no scoped alternative — so an unconditional post-drop purge could destroy unrelated recoverable databases on a shared server, while the `dropped > 0` gate simultaneously failed to reclaim residue from prior runs.

Round 2 resolves both with an explicit, default-off `--purge-dropped` flag, and the delta review (both vendors) is CLEAN-PASS:

- The old `stale==0` early-return is gone; the purge decision is reached on every path and keys on the flag alone — the residue scenario now genuinely purges (end-to-end test verified fail-on-pre-fix, pass-on-fix, against a live dolt-sql-server).
- Without the flag: no purge anywhere, plus a retention hint naming `DOLT_UNDROP` recoverability and the flag. With `--dry-run`: purge is unreachable, with an explicit "ignored" notice when both flags are set.
- Help and output state the server-global, irreversible, removes-undrop-recovery-for-all scope in plain terms.
- Tests pinned at three levels: pure gate table test (incl. residue case), the renamed undrop-recovery observable test (with the no-concurrent-purger assumption documented), and the CLI end-to-end residue test — no shared-server leakage from fixtures.

Thanks @quad341 — the original diagnosis (dropped-DB residue eating disk) was right; it just needed the safety geometry inverted so the destructive half is opt-in.

- `labels` and `wisp_labels` already carry `INDEX idx_labels_label (label)` / `INDEX idx_wisp_labels_label (label)` (migrations 0003, 0021), predating this issue by a month or more. Both tables have `PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label)`, and bd's own label filter builds `id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label = ?)` (internal/storage/dolt/transaction.go, internal/storage/sqlb
NEXT: Categorize as already-implemented/close-candidate. The critical indexed-lookup path this issue asks for (label -> issue_id via labels/wisp_labels, and status filters on issues/wisps) is already present upstream and predates the issue in most cases. The one genuinely novel item (wisps.status+ttl_ts)

Both asks now exist on upstream/main:
1. `bd migrate schema` subcommand landed via PR #4116 (merged 2026-05-26, 9 days after the issue) — cmd/bd/migrate.go:769-820 (migrateSchemaCmd), with `--json` flag and `handleSchemaMigrate()` (cmd/bd/migrate.go:546-610) emitting structured JSON (error keys: no_beads_directory/no_database/unsupported_backend/schema_migration_failed, or {status, applied, latest_version} on success).
2. The core SIGKILL scenario (implicit migration silently running inside an ordinary command) 
DRAFT: This is now implemented on main, independent of the closed PR #4015:

- `bd migrate schema` landed as an explicit, idempotent, `--json`-capable subcommand in #4116 (merged 2026-05-26) — cmd/bd/migrate.go.
- The actual SIGKILL-during-implicit-migration risk you described is addressed more directly than a visible command alone would: once a remote is configured, the remote-migrate gate (default-on since #4551, 2026-07-02) refuses to silently auto-migrate on any store open — `bd ready`, `bd list`,

hema (git log shows 0003 dates to the shared-schema-package commit 2026-04-06; 0021 last touched 2026-05-21, PR #3991), i.e. already present a month-plus before #4314 was filed.
2. bd's own label-filter query builder never does a title LIKE scan for label predicates — it emits an indexed subquery: internal/storage/dolt/transaction.go:463-478 and internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go:162-181 both build `id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label = ?)`. The `LOWER(title) LIKE` pattern the issue describes only exists in bd's free-text search path (transaction.go:323-337, sqlbuild/filter.go:40-54), unrelated to label filtering. `sweepClosedOrderTrackingRetention` does not exist anywhere in bd-main (grep empty) — it's Gas City's own function, not bd code, so any title-LIKE scan is in gascity's query, not bd's.
3. issues.status: originally `idx_issues_status` (0001_create_issues.up.sql), superseded by the composite `idx_issues_status_updated_at (status, updated_at)` in 0052_add_date_indexes.up.sql, merged via PR #3662 on 2026-06-20 (i.e. after #4314 was filed, but independently, as D4v2 perf work) — covers status-only and status+recency filters. Also `idx_issues_lease (status, lease_expires_at)` (0054) and `idx_issues_is_blocked (is_blocked, status)` (0046).
4. wisps.status: `idx_wisps_status (status)` already in 0020_create_wisps.up.sql.
5. wisps(status, ttl_ts): no `ttl_ts` column exists anywhere in bd's schema migrations (grep for 'ttl' across migrations is empty) — this is a Gas City-specific field, presumably tracked in the `metadata` JSON column, not a bd column, so this 
---DRAFT---
Thanks for the detailed writeup. Checking against current main (3ab41958), most of the requested indexes already exist:

- `labels` and `wisp_labels` already carry `INDEX idx_labels_label (label)` / `INDEX idx_wisp_labels_label (label)` (migrations 0003, 0021), predating this issue by a month or more. Both tables have `PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label)`, and bd's own label filter builds `id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label = ?)` (internal/storage/dolt/transaction.go, internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go) — a label-equality lookup is already an index seek, not a `LOWER(title) LIKE` scan. That LIKE pattern only exists in bd's free-text search path, not the label filter; `sweepClosedOrderTrackingRetention` isn't part of the bd codebase, so if it's hitting a title scan, that's in gascity's own query.
- `issues` has `idx_issues_status_updated_at (status, updated_at)` (migration 0052, PR #3662) covering status-only and status+recency filters, plus `idx_issues_lease (status, lease_expires_at)` and `idx_issues_is_blocked (is_blocked, status)`.
- `wisps` already has `idx_wisps_status (status)` (migration 0020).
- The requested `wisps (status, ttl_ts)` index doesn't map onto bd's schema — there's no `ttl_ts` column on `wisps` upstream. That looks like a Gas City-side field (likely in `metadata` JSON), which per the beads charter is exactly the kind of workflow-specific data that should stay outside core schema rather than growing bespoke columns/indexes for one downstream consumer.

Closing as already covered upstream. If the retention sweep is still scanning, worth re-profiling against current main and confirming it's actually filtering through `labels`/`wisp_labels` on `label = ?` rather than a title-based predicate.

S=/tmp/claude-1000/-var-home-matt-dev-mybd/e06b7792-c8f4-4f69-bbd5-ad33ea7ebc53/scratchpad
cat > $S/issue4314-close.md <<'EOF'
Thanks for the detailed writeup. Checked against current main (`3ab41958`) — the indexed-lookup coverage this asks for already exists, most of it predating the issue:

- `labels` / `wisp_labels` carry `INDEX idx_labels_label (label)` and `INDEX idx_wisp_labels_label (label)` (migrations 0003, 0021), and both have `PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label)`. bd's label filter compiles to `id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label = ?)` (internal/storage/sqlbuild/filter.go) — an index seek. The `LOWER(title) LIKE` pattern described here exists only in bd's free-text search path, not label filtering; `sweepClosedOrderTrackingRetention` isn't bd code, so if a title scan is happening it's in the downstream query, not bd's.
- `issues` has `idx_issues_status_updated_at (status, updated_at)` (migration 0052, via #3662) covering status-only and status+recency, plus `idx_issues_lease (status, lease_expires_at)` and `idx_issues_is_blocked (is_blocked, status)`.
- `wisps` has `idx_wisps_status (status)` (migration 0020).
- `wisps (status, ttl_ts)` doesn't map onto bd's schema — no `ttl_ts` column exists upstream (verified by grep across all migrations). That's a downstream field, presumably in `metadata` JSON, which per the project charter should stay outside core schema rather than growing bespoke columns/indexes for one consumer.

Closing as already covered upstream. If the retention sweep still scans, worth re-profiling against current main and confirming it filters through `labels`/`wisp_labels` on `label = ?` rather than a title predicate.

_claude-code-fable-5 on behalf of matt wilkie_
EOF
cat > $S/issue4018-close.md <<'EOF'
Both asks in this use-case doc are now implemented on main, independent of the closed PR the issue referenced:

- `bd migrate schema` landed as an explicit, idempotent, `--json`-capable subcommand via #4116 (merged 2026-05-26): structured JSON with distinct error keys (`no_beads_directory` / `no_database` / `unsupported_backend` / `schema_migration_failed`) and `{status, applied, latest_version}` on success — exit-code-gateable exactly as the orchestrated-upgrade flow here requires.
- The underlying SIGKILL-mid-implicit-migration hazard is addressed at the root: with a remote configured, the remote-migrate gate (default-on since #4551) refuses to auto-migrate on ordinary store opens, so routine commands no longer silently run migrations that an orchestrator might kill; the explicit migration step is the designated migrator's job.

Closing as implemented. Thanks @quad341 — the fleet/deferred-migration scenario write-up here reads like the requirements doc for what #4116 + #4551 became.

_claude-code-fable-5 on behalf of matt wilkie_
EOF
scripts/gh-body-lint $S/issue4314-close.md && gh issue comment 4314 --repo gastownhall/beads --body-file $S/issue4314-close.md && gh issue close 4314 --repo gastownhall/beads --reason completed

**Existing comments:** 2 — (1) coffeegoddd (MEMBER, 2026-05-22): "fixed by #4116"; (2) maphew (2026-06-16, codex-gpt-5.5-medium): a repro-recipe request treating this as a bug report. **Anomaly to flag**: 4016 is a use-case/design-discussion thread, not a bug — the repro-recipe comment applied the wrong triage template. No action taken (read-only task), but the maintainer should know this comment is a misfire before any further triage pass touches this thread.

## What 4016 asks, distinct from sibling 4018

4016's body is a first-person scenario report, not a bare feature request. It asks for:

1. **An explicit `bd migrate schema` subcommand** — same base ask as 4018.
2. **Multi-agent fleet coordination**: "5-rig city" of independent `bd` workspaces, ~15 agent processes reading/writes concurrently; on upgrade, auto-migration races when multiple processes open the same store near-simultaneously.
3. **Determinism/verification across the fleet**: "quiesce the fleet, run the migration once deterministically, verify completion, un-quiesce" — wants confirmation of *when migrations are done* across all rigs, not just a command to run one.
4. **Fit with `bd doctor`'s upgrade-safety story** — explicit question: does/will `bd doctor` cover this?

## Evidence per ask

**1. Explicit subcommand** — Implemented. `cmd/bd/migrate.go:768` `migrateSchemaCmd` ("Apply pending schema migrations (idempotent)"), `--json` flag (migrate.go:825). PR #4116, merged 2026-05-26.

**2. Concurrent-process race on one store (within a rig)** — Implemented for server mode, structurally different for embedded mode:
- `internal/storage/schema/lock.go:56` `MigrateUpWithLock` acquires a named Dolt/MySQL advisory lock (`GET_LOCK`/`RELEASE_LOCK`, `lock.go:74-103`) so concurrent connections serialize on schema migration instead of racing. Wired into `internal/storage/dolt/store.go:1647` and `internal/storage/uow/dolt_sql_provider.go:89` (server / shared-server modes). Landed via PR #4005 "fix(schema): serialize Dolt schema migrations", merged **2026-05-17 22:05 UTC — the same day 4016 was filed** (14:52 UTC), so likely not a direct response to it but resolves the exact symptom described.
- Embedded mode (`internal/storage/embeddeddolt/store.go:379-381`) explicitly does **not** use the named lock — comment: "Embedded mode relies on the dolthub/driver/v2's local file/concurrency controls; schema.MigrateUpWithLock requires a sql-server session lock." It calls bare `schema.MigrateUp`. If quad341's "5-rig" workspaces are embedded-mode (not dolt sql-server), the race protection is delegated to the driver's file locking rather than an explicit bd-level lock — worth a maintainer call on whether that's an adequate answer or a gap.

**3. Cross-rig / cross-clone race + determinism** — Implemented. The remote-migrate gate (`internal/storage/schema/remote_migrate_gate.go`) refuses silent auto-migration on any store that has pending migrations *and* a configured remote (`checkRemoteMigrateGate`, remote_migrate_gate.go:399). Default-on since PR #4551 (merged 2026-07-02, "smart gate on by default", #4516/#4515). It forces exactly the "quiesce, one designated migrator runs it, others adopt" flow quad341 describes:
- `bd migrate --force` / `BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1` = the one designated migrator (migrate.go:41-42, remote_migrate_gate.go:432-462).
- Every other clone runs `bd bootstrap` to adopt (gate options, remote_migrate_gate.go:291-331).
- Smart gate auto-resolves the safe "no one has migrated yet, first-mover" case and auto-fast-forwards a loss-free adopt where provable (remote_migrate_gate.go:483-541).
- Fully documented multi-clone recipe: `docs/getting-started/upgrading.md` §"Remote-backed databases and multiple clones" (lines ~150-215), including the exact push/backup/migrate/push sequencing quad341 asked to orchestrate around.

**Verification of completion** — `bd migrate schema --json` returns a structured status usable per-rig: `{"status": "current"|"applied", "applied": N, "latest_version": N}` (cmd/bd/migrate.go:610-616). This is the primitive for "verify completion" after unquiescing each rig.

**4. Fit with `bd doctor`** — Partially answered, not a discrete "N schema migrations pending" doctor check:
- `cmd/bd/doctor/migration.go` `CheckPendingMigrations`/`DetectPendingMigrations` — covers **git-hook** migration only, not schema version.
- `cmd/bd/doctor/migration_skew.go` `CheckMigrationContentSkew` — read-only diagnostic detecting cross-clone schema *fork* (#4259 divergence), compares local vs. cached remote-tracking ref; never gates push/pull, just warns.
- No `bd doctor` check directly answers "is schema migration pending/complete" the way `bd migrate schema --json` does — that gap is real but narrow (the JSON status flag already serves the stated need).

## Categorization

**Already-implemented** (core ask + the multi-agent/fleet coordination scenarios), with one caveat worth surfacing to the maintainer rather than silently closing: the embedded-mode concurrency story is driver-delegated, not an explicit bd-level lock like server mode has, and `bd doctor` has no discrete schema-pending check parallel to the hook/skew ones it does have.

Recommend: **close-candidate**, but flag the embedded-mode lock question and the stray repro-recipe comment for maintainer awareness first (this task took no action per read-only instructions).

## Draft close comment (evidence-only, not posted)

> Following up on the use case here — the pieces you asked about have since landed:
>
> - **Explicit `bd migrate schema`**: #4116 (merged 2026-05-26), idempotent, supports `--json` returning `{status, applied, latest_version}` for scripted verification.
> - **Concurrent-process race on one store**: server/shared-server mode now serializes schema migration behind a named advisory lock (`GET_LOCK`/`RELEASE_LOCK`) — #4005 (merged 2026-05-17). Embedded-mode stores rely on the Dolt embedded driver's own file/concurrency controls rather than this lock.
> - **Cross-rig coordination / determinism**: the remote-migrate gate is on by default since #4551 (2026-07-02) — any store with pending migrations and a configured remote refuses silent auto-migration and requires an explicit "one designated migrator runs `bd migrate --force` + `bd dolt push`, every other clone runs `bd bootstrap`" flow, with a smart-gate auto-resolve for the safe first-mover case. Documented end-to-end in [docs/getting-started/upgrading.md § Remote-backed databases and multiple clones](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/getting-started/upgrading.md#remote-backed-databases-and-multiple-clones).
>
> `bd doctor` doesn't yet have a discrete "N schema migrations pending" check parallel to its hook-migration and cross-clone-skew checks — flagging that as the one open thread if it matters for your `bd doctor` integration. Let us know if the embedded-mode locking story needs to be more explicit for your setup.

attachment.snippet:
396	housekeeping section.
397	
398	`verify-next` creates a clean detached worktree under
399	`<mybd-root>/.worktrees/beads/verify-*`, runs the recorded
400	`verify_cmd`, stores logs under `.worktrees/beads/.verify-logs/`, and writes
401	`verify_state=passed|failed` plus result metadata back to bd. Keep full-suite
402	concurrency low by default; Beads/Dolt tests are process and disk heavy.
403	
404	### PR Merge Tails (babysitter pattern)
405	
406	Never babysit CI in-session. When a PR's remaining work is "merge when checks
407	pass", run:
408	
409	```bash
410	scripts/pr-handoff <pr-number> [--repo owner/repo] [--method squash|merge|rebase] [--no-flake-rerun] [--bead <id>]
411	```
412	
413	and end the session. The `pr-babysit` systemd user timer (installed via
414	`scripts/install-pr-babysit`, fires every 12 min, zero model tokens) then owns
415	the merge: it merges green PRs after a blocking `pr-preflight` base-health
416	check, reruns failed jobs once when flake-rerun is allowed, and on persistent
417	failure relabels the bead `merge-blocked` and unclaims it so `bd ready`
418	surfaces it for agent judgment. Patrol log:
419	`~/.local/state/pr-babysit/patrol.log`.
420	
421	Role split, not claims: `bd update --claim` is idempotent for the same user,
422	and all local sessions run as the same user, so claims cannot mutually exclude
423	parallel sessions (2026-07-24: one session merged gastownhall/beads#4942 with a
424	red nix job while another was mid-review of it, breaking main's nix build for
425	~2h). The rule that prevents this: sessions produce (review, fix, push, hand
426	off); only the patrol merges. Merging in-session is allowed only when checks
427	are already decisively green at the moment of action and no `merge-when-green`
428	bead exists for that PR.
429	
430	## Quick Reference
431	
432	```bash
433	bd ready              # Find available work
434	bd show <id>          # View issue details
435	bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work atomically
436	bd close <id>         # Complete work
437	bd dolt push          # Push beads data to remote

input.description: Inspect pr-handoff usage and timer status

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Hand a PR's merge tail to the pr-babysit patrol instead of watching CI in-session.
#
# Creates-or-claims the tracking bead for the PR, labels it merge-when-green,
# and records the merge policy in the bead notes. The pr-babysit timer (see
# scripts/pr-babysit) then owns the merge: sessions that run this script must
# NOT merge, close, or re-run checks on the PR afterwards.
#
# Usage: scripts/pr-handoff <pr-number> [--repo owner/repo] [--method squash|merge|rebase] [--no-flake-rerun] [--bead <id>]
#
# When several beads share the PR's external ref (e.g. a tracking bead plus a
# follow-ups bead), pass --bead to name the merge-tail bead explicitly; the
# ref lookup otherwise takes the first match.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
REPO="gastownhall/beads"
METHOD="squash"
FLAKE_RERUN="allowed"
PR=""
BEAD_OVERRIDE=""

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --repo) REPO="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --method) METHOD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --bead) BEAD_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --no-flake-rerun) FLAKE_RERUN="no"; shift ;;
    -*) echo "unknown flag: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    *) PR="$1"; shift ;;
  esac
done
[[ -n "$PR" ]] || { echo "usage: pr-handoff <pr-number> [--repo owner/repo] [--method m] [--no-flake-rerun]" >&2; exit 2; }

cd "$ROOT"

title="$(gh pr view "$PR" --repo "$REPO" --json title,state --jq '.title')" || { echo "cannot read PR $PR on $REPO" >&2; exit 1; }

# Find an existing bead under either external-ref convention.
bead="$BEAD_OVERRIDE"
active
